title: 2006: God, the Son
content: The co-equal and co-eternal Son of the Father, who became incarnate in Jesus Christ for the redemption of the world. Scripture stresses both the divinity and the humanity of the incarnate Son of God, and the necessity and total sufficiency of his atoning death for human redemption. Through his resurrection and ascension, he interecedes for believers at the right hand of God.

title: 2009: Jesus Christ, anger of
content:
Jesus Christ's controlled emotion arising from his unswerving opposition to evil and his determination to eradicate it.

Causes of Jesus Christ's anger

Petty legalism in religious observance

MAT 15:3, MAT 23:1–23:4; ; MRK 3:4–3:5

Attempts to prevent access to him

MRK 10:14

title: 2012: Jesus Christ, authority of
content:
The right of Jesus Christ to speak and act on his Father's behalf in forgiving sin, pronouncing judgment and promising eternal life to those who believe in him.

Jesus Christ's authority underlines his divine status

JHN 8:28 See also JHN 8:58, JHN 14:6; 1CO 1:24; COL 2:10

The origin of Jesus Christ's authority

It is given by God the Father

JHN 3:35 See also MAT 28:18; LUK 10:22; JHN 5:26–5:27, JHN 7:16, JHN 10:18, JHN 12:49–12:50, JHN 17:2

It is limited by the Father

MRK 10:40 See also MAT 24:36 parallel MRK 13:32; PHP 2:6–2:8

It is questioned by others

MAT 12:24–12:28 parallels MRK 3:22–3:26, LUK 11:15–11:20 See also MAT 21:23–21:27 parallels MRK 11:27–11:33, LUK 20:1–20:8; JHN 2:18

The authority of Jesus Christ is seen in his works

He has authority over nature

MAT 8:14–8:15 parallels MRK 1:30–1:31, LUK 4:38–4:39; ; MAT 20:29–20:34 parallels MRK 10:46–10:52, LUK 18:35–18:43; ; ACT 3:16, ACT 4:30

MRK 1:23–1:27 parallel LUK 4:33–4:36; ; LUK 10:17

He has authority over death

MAT 9:18–9:25 parallels MRK 5:22–5:42, LUK 8:40–8:55; ; MAT 28:1–28:7 parallels MRK 16:1–16:6, LUK 24:1–24:8, JHN 20:1–20:17; ; LUK 7:11–7:16; ; JHN 11:38–11:44

The authority of Jesus Christ is seen in his teaching

MAT 7:28–7:29 See also MAT 22:33, MAT 22:46; MRK 1:22, MRK 1:27 parallel LUK 4:32

His authority is questioned and opposed by others

MAT 26:65–26:68; ; LUK 4:28–4:30

Jesus Christ delegates his authority to others

His followers preach in his name

MAT 10:7, MAT 28:18–28:20

His followers continue his works

MAT 10:1 parallels MRK 6:7, LUK 9:1–9:2; ; MAT 10:8; ; MRK 3:15, MRK 6:13, MRK 16:17–16:18; ; LUK 10:1–10:12; ; JHN 14:12; ; ACT 3:6, ACT 3:16

MAT 18:18; ; MRK 6:11; ; LUK 22:28–22:30; ; JHN 20:22–20:23; ; 1CO 6:2–6:3

JHN 5:22–5:23 See also JHN 11:4, JHN 17:5

He is opposed

MAT 12:14 parallels MRK 3:6, LUK 6:11

title: 2015: Jesus Christ, compassion of
content:
Jesus Christ's pity and loving concern for the lowly and the needy. His words and deeds show God's merciful and gracious nature in action.

Jesus Christ shows the compassion of God

2CO 1:3 See also 2CH 36:15; PSA 86:15; HOS 11:4, HOS 11:8–11:9; LUK 1:72, LUK 1:78, LUK 15:20

The compassion of Jesus Christ is the basis of Christian confidence

HEB 4:14–4:16 The Greek word here translated “sympathise” has the sense of “be compassionate towards”.

The demonstration of Jesus Christ's compassion

In supporting the weak

MAT 12:20 See also ISA 42:3; MAT 19:14 parallels MRK 10:14, LUK 18:16

In healing the sick

MAT 14:14 parallel LUK 9:11 See also MAT 20:34 parallels MRK 10:52, LUK 18:42–18:43; MRK 1:41 parallels MAT 8:3, LUK 5:13; LUK 13:12

In comforting the bereaved

LUK 7:13 See also LUK 8:50 parallel MRK 5:36; JHN 11:33–11:35, JHN 19:25–19:27, JHN 20:14–20:16

In feeding the hungry

MAT 15:32 parallel MRK 8:2–8:3 See also MAT 14:16 parallels MRK 6:37, LUK 9:13, JHN 6:5–6:6

In finding and forgiving lost sinners

MAT 9:36 parallel MRK 6:34 See also ISA 40:11; MAT 18:14, MAT 23:37–23:38 parallel LUK 13:34–13:35; LUK 7:47–7:48; JHN 8:10–8:11

In giving rest to those who are burdened or abandoned

MAT 11:28–11:29 See also MRK 1:40–1:41 parallels MAT 8:1–8:3, LUK 5:12–5:13; LUK 11:46, LUK 15:1–15:2, LUK 17:12–17:14

Jesus Christ's compassion is a model for Christians to follow

LUK 10:36–10:37 See also JHN 13:34, JHN 17:18; PHP 2:1

title: 2018: Jesus Christ, divinity of
content:
The equality and identity of Jesus Christ as God is clearly stated in the NT, and is also implied by the words and deeds of Jesus Christ. The OT prophecies also point to the divinity of the coming Messiah.

The NT writers affirm Jesus Christ's divinity

HEB 1:8 See also PSA 45:6; JHN 1:1–1:2, JHN 1:18; ROM 9:5 NIV footnote has alternative translations: “Christ who is over all. God be for ever praised!” Or: “Christ. God who is over all be for ever praised!”; PHP 2:6; TIT 2:13; 2PE 1:1

Statements which imply Jesus Christ's divinity

MAT 1:23 See also ISA 7:14; LUK 1:35; COL 1:15, COL 2:2, COL 2:9; 1TI 1:17; 1JN 5:20

Jesus Christ's unity with the Father and the Holy Spirit in the Godhead

MAT 28:19 See also JHN 14:16; 2CO 13:14; EPH 1:13–1:14, EPH 2:18, EPH 2:22, EPH 3:14–3:17, EPH 4:4–4:6

Jesus Christ's eternal nature indicates his divinity

Jesus Christ precedes creation

COL 1:17 See also MIC 5:2; JHN 17:5, JHN 17:24; 2TI 1:9; 1PE 1:20; 1JN 1:1, 1JN 2:13

Jesus Christ is everlasting

JHN 8:58 See also HEB 1:12; PSA 102:27; HEB 7:3, HEB 7:24, HEB 13:8; REV 1:8, REV 5:13, REV 22:13

Jesus Christ's pre-existence indicates his divinity

JHN 6:62 See also JHN 3:13, JHN 3:31, JHN 6:41–6:42, JHN 13:3, JHN 16:28

Jesus Christ's manifestation of God's glory indicates his divinity

HEB 1:3 See also MAT 17:2 parallels MRK 9:2–9:3, LUK 9:29; JHN 1:14; 1CO 2:8; 2CO 4:4; JAS 2:1

Jesus Christ's divinity in the OT

The divinity of the coming Messiah

ISA 9:6, ISA 40:3; ; JER 23:6; ; MAL 3:1

NT passages which apply OT passages about God to Jesus Christ

ROM 10:13 See also JOL 2:32; JHN 12:40–12:41; ISA 6:10; ROM 9:33; ISA 8:14; EPH 4:8; PSA 68:18

Jesus Christ's claims to divinity

He claimed to be one with the Father

JHN 5:17–5:18 See also JHN 10:30–10:33, JHN 10:36–10:38, JHN 12:45, JHN 14:7, JHN 14:9–14:11, JHN 17:11, JHN 17:21

He demonstrated his authority to forgive sin

LUK 5:20–5:24 parallels MAT 9:2–9:6, MRK 2:5–2:10 See also LUK 7:47–7:48

Jesus Christ's actions imply his divinity

MAT 8:26–8:27 parallels MRK 4:39–4:41, LUK 8:24–8:25 See also MAT 12:8 parallels MRK 2:28, LUK 6:5; LUK 8:39 parallel MRK 5:19–5:20

Jesus Christ's resurrection confirms his divinity

ACT 2:36 See also ROM 1:4; PHP 2:9–2:11

Jesus Christ's names and titles point to his divinity

Jesus Christ as judge

JHN 5:27 In the OT, final responsibility for judgment is assigned to God. See also MAT 25:31–25:33; MRK 8:38 parallels MAT 16:27, LUK 9:26; ACT 17:31; ROM 2:16; 2CO 5:10

Jesus Christ as ``I am''

JHN 11:25 “I am” is the meaning of God's name in the OT (see Ex 3:14). See also JHN 6:35, JHN 8:12, JHN 10:7, JHN 10:11, JHN 14:6, JHN 15:1, JHN 18:5–18:6

Jesus Christ as Saviour

ACT 5:31 According to the OT, God alone can save. See also ACT 4:12; EPH 5:23; HEB 7:25

Jesus Christ as Lord

ROM 10:9 “Lord” was equivalent to God's name in the OT. See also LUK 1:43, LUK 2:11; JHN 13:13; 1CO 12:3; 2CO 4:5; REV 19:16

Jesus Christ as creator

COL 1:16 See also JHN 1:3, JHN 1:10; ACT 3:15; ROM 11:36; 1CO 8:6; HEB 1:2, HEB 1:10; PSA 102:25

Jesus Christ as shepherd

HEB 13:20 “Shepherd” was a well-known OT name for God. See also JHN 10:11–10:16; 1PE 2:25, 1PE 5:4

Those who recognised Jesus Christ's divinity

The disciples

JHN 20:28 See also MAT 16:16 parallels MRK 8:29, LUK 9:20

The demons

MRK 3:11; ; LUK 4:41 parallel MRK 1:34

The consequences of recognising Jesus Christ's divinity

Jesus Christ is worshipped as God

LUK 24:52 The fact that no-one but God may be worshipped is fundamental to Judaism. See also MAT 2:11, MAT 28:9, MAT 28:17; JHN 9:38; 2TI 4:18; 2PE 3:18; REV 1:5–1:6, REV 5:12–5:13, REV 7:10

Prayer is addressed to Jesus Christ

ACT 7:59–7:60, ACT 9:13; ; 1CO 16:22; ; REV 22:20

resource.label: 2203: Jesus Christ, titles and names of

title: 2021: Jesus Christ, faithfulness of
content:
The total reliability and constancy of Jesus Christ is shown in his personal character, and made known by his words and works.

Jesus Christ's faithfulness is seen in his character

He is faithful by nature

REV 19:11 A description of the exalted Christ. See also REV 1:5, REV 3:14

The promised Messiah is faithful

ISA 11:5 See also ISA 42:3

Jesus Christ is faithful because he never changes

HEB 13:8 See also HEB 1:11–1:12; PSA 102:26–102:27

Jesus Christ is faithful because he is the truth

JHN 1:14 “grace” and “truth” are often associated in Hebrew with love and faithfulness. See also JHN 1:17, JHN 14:6

Jesus Christ's faithfulness demonstrated in his obedience

He is faithful to his Father

HEB 3:6 See also LUK 2:49; JHN 5:30, JHN 6:38, JHN 8:29, JHN 14:31; HEB 3:2

He kept faithfully to his work

LUK 4:43 parallel MRK 1:38 See also JHN 4:34, JHN 9:4, JHN 12:27, JHN 17:4, JHN 19:30; HEB 2:17–2:18

Jesus Christ's faithfulness is seen in his words

In his predictions about events

MAT 16:28 parallels LUK 9:27, MRK 9:1 This prediction could refer to either Jesus Christ's transfiguration or his ascension; MAT 24:2 parallels MRK 13:2, LUK 21:6 This prediction was fulfilled in A.D. 70 when the Romans destroyed Jerusalem and the temple. See also MAT 21:2 parallels MRK 11:2, LUK 19:30 finding a donkey and her colt; MAT 26:21 parallels MRK 14:18, LUK 22:21, JHN 13:21 Jesus Christ's betrayer; MAT 26:34 parallels MRK 14:30, LUK 22:34, JHN 13:38 Peter's denial; JHN 16:20, JHN 21:18–21:19

In his promises about eternal life

JHN 5:24 See also MAT 19:28–19:29 parallels MRK 10:29–10:30, LUK 18:29–18:30; LUK 23:43; JHN 6:47, JHN 6:53–6:54, JHN 8:51

In his promise of authority for believers

MAT 17:20 See also MAT 16:19, MAT 18:18, MAT 21:21 parallel MRK 11:23; MAT 28:18–28:19; LUK 10:19

In his promise about the Holy Spirit

JHN 14:26 See also LUK 24:49; JHN 14:16, JHN 15:26, JHN 16:7; ACT 1:4, ACT 1:8, ACT 2:38–2:39, ACT 10:44–10:46; ROM 5:5; 1CO 12:7

Jesus Christ's promises about the future are faithful

JHN 14:2–14:3 See also MAT 16:27, MAT 26:64; JHN 14:28

Jesus Christ is faithful to believers in all circumstances

2TI 2:13 See also MAT 18:20, MAT 28:20; JHN 17:9; ACT 18:9–18:10; 2TH 3:3; HEB 10:23

title: 2024: Jesus Christ, glory of
content:
Jesus Christ's radiance and splendour reflects the glory of the Father, and is both revealed to and reflected in the lives of believers.

Jesus Christ's glory existed before the incarnation

JHN 17:5 See also JHN 1:14, JHN 17:24

Jesus Christ's glory is God's glory

God's glory is reflected in Jesus Christ

HEB 1:3 See also JHN 12:41 John makes no distinction between God's glory revealed in Isa 6:3 and the glory of Jesus Christ; JHN 13:32; 2CO 4:4, 2CO 4:6

Jesus Christ brings glory to his Father

JHN 14:13 See also JHN 13:31, JHN 17:1, JHN 17:4; ROM 16:27; EPH 1:12; JUD 1:25

The Holy Spirit glorifies Jesus Christ

JHN 16:14

Jesus Christ's glory is revealed on earth

Through his miracles

JHN 2:11 See also JHN 11:4, JHN 11:40

To his disciples

LUK 9:28–9:32 parallels MAT 17:1–17:2, MRK 9:2–9:3 See also JHN 1:14; ACT 9:3, ACT 22:6, ACT 26:13; 2PE 1:17

In his death and resurrection

JHN 12:23 One of the major themes of John's Gospel is that Jesus Christ is glorified through his death and resurrection. See also JHN 7:39, JHN 12:16; ACT 3:13–3:15; 1PE 1:21; HEB 2:9

The glory of the exalted Christ

His appearance

REV 1:13–1:16 See also ACT 7:55–7:56; REV 2:18, REV 19:11–19:16

He receives glory from all creation

REV 5:13 See also HEB 13:21; 2PE 3:18; REV 1:6, REV 5:11–5:12, REV 7:9–7:12

Jesus Christ's glory will be revealed at the second coming

MAT 16:27 parallels MRK 8:38, LUK 9:26 See also MAT 24:27, MAT 24:30 parallels MRK 13:26, LUK 21:27; MAT 25:31; 2TH 1:7, 2TH 2:8; TIT 2:13; 1PE 4:13

Jesus Christ's glory is shared by believers

As they become like him

2CO 3:18 See also JHN 17:22; COL 1:27

At the end of time

1CO 15:49 See also ROM 8:17–8:18; PHP 3:21; COL 3:4

title: 2027: Jesus Christ, grace and mercy of
content:
The qualities of Jesus Christ by which he is compassionate, accepting and generous. In his ministry, Christ demonstrates these qualities towards those whom he encounters. Believers should model themselves upon Jesus Christ in this respect.

The grace and mercy of God made known in Jesus Christ

JHN 1:14, JHN 1:16–1:17 See also LUK 2:40, LUK 2:52; 2CO 8:9; TIT 2:11 Jesus Christ shows the grace of God in his life.

Grace and mercy in the ministry of Jesus Christ

Jesus Christ responds to pleas for mercy

MAT 9:27–9:30 See also MAT 15:22, MAT 20:29–20:34 parallels MRK 10:46–10:52, LUK 18:35–18:42

His teaching reflects his grace and mercy

LUK 6:35–6:36 See also HOS 6:6; MAT 6:12–6:15 parallel LUK 11:4; MAT 9:10–9:13 parallels MRK 2:15–2:17, LUK 5:29–5:32; MAT 18:21–18:35 the parable of the unmerciful servant; LUK 10:30–10:37 the parable of the good Samaritan; EPH 4:32

Grace and mercy in relation to salvation

The grace of Jesus Christ

ACT 15:11 See also ROM 3:24, ROM 5:15; EPH 2:4–2:5, EPH 2:8; 2TI 1:9

The mercy of Jesus Christ

LUK 23:34 See also MAT 9:2 parallels MRK 2:3–2:5, LUK 5:18–5:20; LUK 7:37–7:38, LUK 7:48; ROM 5:6, ROM 5:15; 1TI 1:13–1:14 Paul speaks about his persecution of the Christians prior to his conversion experience.

Eternal life is a gift of grace and mercy

ROM 5:21 See also ROM 6:23; TIT 3:7

The grace and mercy of Jesus Christ and the Christian life

The need to rely upon the grace of Jesus Christ

2CO 12:9; 2TI 2:1 See also EPH 4:7; 2PE 3:18; HEB 4:16

Prayers for the grace of Jesus Christ

ROM 1:7 See also ROM 16:20; 1CO 16:23; GAL 1:3, GAL 6:18; EPH 6:24; PHP 4:23; 2JN 1:3; REV 22:21

title: 2030: Jesus Christ, holiness of
content:
The holiness of Jesus Christ is seen in his divine nature and work, as he stands apart from and above the created world with divine power, authority and purity. Recognition of the holiness of Jesus Christ leads both to a realisation of sin and unworthiness and to worship and adoration.

The holy character of Jesus Christ

Its divine origin

LUK 1:35 See also JHN 1:1–1:2, JHN 3:31, JHN 8:23, JHN 13:3, JHN 17:14, JHN 17:16

Its divine nature

COL 2:9 See also JHN 1:14, JHN 10:30, JHN 10:38, JHN 14:10; PHP 2:6; HEB 1:3

Its divine purity

HEB 7:26 See also 2CO 5:21; HEB 4:15; 1PE 1:19, 1PE 2:22; 1JN 3:3, 1JN 3:5

Its divine power

ACT 4:30 See also ACT 10:38

The holy work of Jesus Christ

He is set apart as God's servant

ACT 4:27 See also MRK 10:45 parallel MAT 20:28; JHN 14:31; ACT 3:26; PHP 2:7–2:8; HEB 10:7

His life is consecrated to the will and purpose of God

MAT 26:39 parallels MRK 14:35–14:36, LUK 22:42 See also MAT 26:42; JHN 12:49–12:50, JHN 14:31

He is appointed as the judge of sinners

JHN 5:22, JHN 5:26–5:27; ; ACT 17:31; ; 2CO 5:10

He makes God's people holy

HEB 13:12 See also JHN 17:19; EPH 5:25–5:27; HEB 2:11, HEB 10:10, HEB 10:14; 1PE 2:4–2:5, 1PE 2:9–2:10

Declarations of the holiness of Jesus Christ

By David: PSA 16:10; ; ACT 2:27, ACT 13:35; MRK 1:24 parallel LUK 4:34 by demons; ; LUK 1:35 by the angel Gabriel; JHN 6:69; ; ACT 3:14; REV 3:7 by Jesus Christ himself

Results of recognising the holiness of Jesus Christ

Awareness of sin and unworthiness

LUK 5:8 See also MAT 8:8 parallel LUK 7:6–7:7

Fear

MAT 8:28–8:34 parallels MRK 5:9–5:17, LUK 8:26–8:37; ; REV 1:17

Adoration and worship

REV 5:8–5:14

title: 2033: Jesus Christ, humanity of
content:
Scripture stresses the total humanity of Jesus Christ. Although sinless, Christ shared in the general condition of humanity, including suffering and death.

Jesus Christ was a man

By his own claims

JHN 8:40 See also MAT 8:20 parallel LUK 9:58

In statements made by others

JHN 19:5 See also MAT 8:27; MRK 15:39 parallel LUK 23:47 the centurion; LUK 15:2 the Pharisees and teachers of the law; LUK 23:18; JHN 1:33, JHN 4:42, JHN 7:12, JHN 7:15 some Jews; JHN 7:27, JHN 9:33, JHN 11:47; ACT 2:22–2:23

Jesus Christ shared in the general condition of humanity

He assumed human nature

JHN 1:14 “flesh” here speaks not only of a material body but also of human nature. See also PHP 2:7–2:8; HEB 2:14; 1JN 4:2

He had a human descent

ROM 1:3 See also MAT 1:1, MAT 13:54–13:56 parallel MRK 6:2–6:3; ROM 9:5

He had a normal body which could be handled and touched

1JN 1:1 See also MAT 14:36 parallel MRK 6:56; LUK 2:28; 1TI 3:16

He had a soul

MAT 26:38 parallel MRK 14:34 See also ISA 53:11

Jesus Christ partook of human experience

He was born

GAL 4:4 See also LUK 2:6–2:7

He grew and developed

LUK 2:40, LUK 2:52

He was hungry

MAT 21:18 parallel MRK 11:12 See also MAT 4:2 parallel LUK 4:2

He was thirsty

JHN 19:28 See also JHN 4:7

He was tired

JHN 4:6

He slept

See also MAT 8:24 parallels MRK 4:38, LUK 8:23

He was tempted

HEB 2:18 See also MAT 4:1 parallels MRK 1:13, LUK 4:2; HEB 4:15

He suffered

1PE 4:1 See also COL 1:22; HEB 5:7–5:8, HEB 10:10; 1PE 2:24

He died

MAT 27:50 parallels MRK 15:37, LUK 23:46, JHN 19:30 Jesus Christ most closely identified with the rest of humanity through his death. However Jesus Christ is distinct from human beings in that his death was an act of free will and not the inevitable consequence of sin (see Jn 10:17–18). See also HEB 2:9, HEB 2:14

The risen Christ has human characteristics

LUK 24:37–24:43 See also JHN 20:27

The returning Christ retains his human identity

ACT 1:11

Jesus Christ was a sinless human being

HEB 4:15 Sinlessness does not make Jesus Christ less human. It is sin that degrades humanity. See also LUK 23:41, LUK 23:47; HEB 7:26

The necessity of Jesus Christ's humanity

As a man Jesus Christ made atonement for sin

ROM 5:18–5:19 See also ROM 5:12–5:17; 1TI 2:5; HEB 2:17

As a man Jesus Christ gives the gift of resurrection

1CO 15:21 See also 1CO 15:45; PHP 3:20–3:21

As a man Jesus Christ is able to help others in their need

HEB 2:18 See also HEB 4:15–5:2

As a man Jesus Christ is the perfect example

PHP 2:5–2:8

title: 2036: Jesus Christ, humility of
content:
The obedient submission of Jesus Christ to his Father, seen in his willingness to become a human being for humanity's sake, his freedom from self-interest and his willingness to serve others.

Jesus Christ's willingness to become a human being

title: 2078: Jesus Christ, sonship of
content:
Jesus Christ is described as Son of David, Son of Man and Son of God. His sonship speaks of his divinity and humanity and is closely associated with his role as promised Messiah, suffering Saviour, risen Lord and coming judge and king. It also speaks of his intimate, obedient and unique relationship with his Father and of his mission to enable people to become children of God through faith in him.

Others affirm Jesus Christ is God's Son

MAT 14:33 See also MRK 15:39 parallel MAT 27:54; JHN 1:34; ROM 1:4; HEB 4:14; 2JN 1:3

Demons affirm Jesus Christ is God's Son

MRK 5:7 parallels MAT 8:29, LUK 8:28 See also MRK 3:11; LUK 4:41

``Son of God'' as a taunt

By the devil in the wilderness: MAT 4:3 parallel LUK 4:3; ; MAT 4:6 parallel LUK 4:9; MAT 27:40, MAT 27:43

``Son of God'' stresses Jesus Christ's deity

Jesus Christ is the Son of God the Father

JHN 3:35 In John's Gospel in particular, the terms Son/Father are used many times to speak of Jesus Christ's unique relationship with God. See also MAT 11:27 parallel LUK 10:22; MAT 24:36 parallel MRK 13:32; JHN 5:20–5:22; HEB 1:1–1:2; 1JN 1:3, 1JN 2:22–2:23; 2JN 1:9

``Son of God'' implies Jesus Christ's equality with God

JHN 5:18 See also JHN 5:23, JHN 10:30, JHN 10:33, JHN 14:9–14:10

``Son of God'' implies Jesus Christ's obedience to the Father

JHN 5:19 See also MAT 26:39 parallels MRK 14:36, LUK 22:42; MAT 26:42; JHN 8:28, JHN 12:49, JHN 14:31, JHN 15:10; ROM 5:19; HEB 5:8, HEB 10:9

Jesus Christ as ``Son of God'' is the Messiah

``Son of God'' as a Messianic title for Jesus Christ

MAT 26:63–26:64 parallels MRK 14:61–14:62, LUK 22:70–23:2 See also MAT 16:16; MRK 1:1; LUK 1:32, LUK 4:41; JHN 1:49, JHN 11:27

``Son of God'' as one sent by God

GAL 4:4 See also MAT 21:37 parallels MRK 12:6, LUK 20:13; JHN 3:16–3:17; ROM 8:3, ROM 8:32; 1JN 4:9

Grounds for regarding Jesus Christ as the Son of God

The virgin birth

LUK 1:35

Jesus Christ's resurrection

ROM 1:3–1:4

Jesus Christ's works

JHN 10:36–10:38 See also JHN 5:17, JHN 5:19, JHN 5:36, JHN 14:11

Jesus Christ's oneness with the Father

JHN 10:30 See also JHN 12:45, JHN 14:10, JHN 17:21

Jesus Christ as Son of God is the object of the church's faith

JHN 6:40 See also JHN 3:36, JHN 20:31; ACT 8:37; GAL 2:20; 1JN 3:23, 1JN 4:15, 1JN 5:5, 1JN 5:13

Jesus Christ as Son of God is the content of the church's gospel

ROM 1:9 See also ACT 9:20; 2CO 1:19; 1JN 5:9–5:12

title: 2221: Jesus Christ, Son of Man
content:
Jesus Christ's preferred title for himself. The term points to the humanity and servanthood of Christ, but also reflects Daniel's vision of the son of man as a coming figure of judgment and authority.

Jesus Christ as the Son of Man in his human nature

OT use of son of man'' to meanhuman being''

NUM 23:19 See also JOB 25:6, JOB 35:8; PSA 8:4, PSA 80:17, PSA 144:3

Jesus Christ describes himself as Son of Man to stress his humanity

MAT 8:20 parallel LUK 9:58 See also MAT 11:19 parallel LUK 7:34; MAT 16:13, MAT 16:15; LUK 6:22; JHN 9:35

Blasphemy against the Son of Man is forgivable

MAT 12:32 See also LUK 12:10

Jesus Christ as the Son of Man in his prophetic ministry

The OT use of ``son of man'' as a title for Ezekiel

EZK 2:1

Jesus Christ preaches God's word

MAT 13:37–13:43

Jesus Christ offers the words of eternal life

JHN 6:27 See also JHN 6:53, JHN 6:63, JHN 6:68

Jesus Christ as the Son of Man in his suffering and death

Jesus Christ as a servant

MAT 20:28 parallel MRK 10:45

Jesus Christ came to suffer, die and be raised

LUK 24:6–24:7 See also MAT 12:40 parallel LUK 11:30 the sign of Jonah; MAT 17:9 parallel MRK 9:9 the transfigurationMAT 17:22–17:23 parallels MRK 9:31, LUK 9:44; MAT 20:18 parallels MRK 10:33, LUK 18:31; MAT 26:24 parallels MRK 14:21, LUK 22:22; MRK 8:31 parallel LUK 9:22; JHN 3:14, JHN 12:23, JHN 13:31

Jesus Christ as the Son of Man in his authority and dominion

The vision of the son of man

DAN 7:13–7:14 This passage provides the main OT background to Jesus Christ's understanding of himself as the “Son of Man”. His main uses of the title emphasise his dominion and authority, and also paradoxically that this glory will be attained through the humiliation of the cross.

Son of man meaning ``son of Adam''

GEN 1:26 Jesus Christ as Son of Man (in the Hebrew “son of Adam”) inherits the promise originally given to Adam of dominion over creation, which Adam forfeited because of his fall into sin. See also ROM 5:14; 1CO 15:45; HEB 2:6–2:9; PSA 8:4–8:6

Jesus Christ had authority on earth

MAT 9:6 parallels MRK 2:10, LUK 5:24 Authority to forgive sins. See also MAT 12:8 parallels MRK 2:28, LUK 6:5 authority over the Sabbath

Jesus Christ is now ascended into glory

ACT 7:55–7:56 See also REV 1:13, REV 14:14

Jesus Christ will come to judge and reign over all

MAT 16:27–16:28 parallels MRK 8:38, LUK 9:26 See also MAT 10:23, MAT 24:30 parallels MRK 13:26, LUK 21:27 the second coming; MAT 24:44 parallel LUK 12:40 the day and hour unknown; MAT 26:64 parallels MRK 14:62, LUK 22:69 Jesus Christ before the Sanhedrin; LUK 18:8, LUK 21:36; JHN 5:27

title: 2224: Jesus Christ, the Lord
content:
A title that signifies Jesus Christ's absolute authority and the basis on which people may know him. It is especially associated with his resurrection and return.

Lord as a title of respect for the earthly Jesus Christ

MAT 8:25 See also MAT 15:25, MAT 15:27 parallel MRK 7:28; MAT 21:3 parallels MRK 11:3, LUK 19:31

Lord as a mark of Jesus Christ's authority as a teacher

1TH 4:15 See also ACT 11:16, ACT 20:35; 1CO 7:10

Lord as a sign of Jesus Christ's divinity

Jesus Christ's divinity

JHN 20:28 “Lord” is a common divine title in the Greek OT and is instinctively transferred to Jesus Christ in the NT. See also 1CO 8:6; 2CO 3:17–3:18; EPH 4:5; 2TH 2:16; 2PE 1:2

The day of Christ Jesus

PHP 1:6 The “day of Christ Jesus” is equivalent to the OT “day of the LORD”. See also 1CO 1:8; 2CO 1:14; PHP 1:10, PHP 2:16; REV 1:10

Jesus Christ is ruler over all

REV 17:14 See also DEU 10:17; REV 19:16

Jesus Christ is the Saviour

ROM 10:13 See also JOL 2:32; ACT 2:21

Jesus Christ is Lord of the Sabbath

MAT 12:8 parallels MRK 2:28, LUK 6:5

Jesus the Messiah is Lord

ACT 2:36 “Christ” is the Greek equivalent of the Hebrew word “Messiah”. See also 2CO 4:5; COL 3:24

``Jesus is Lord'' is the basic Christian statement of faith

ROM 10:9 See also ACT 11:20, ACT 16:31, ACT 20:21; 1CO 12:3; 2CO 4:5; PHP 2:10–2:11

Jesus is Lord because of the resurrection

ROM 1:4 See also ACT 4:33; ROM 4:24, ROM 14:9; HEB 13:20

Lord is a natural title for the risen Christ

LUK 24:34 See also LUK 22:61; ACT 1:21, ACT 7:59; 1CO 9:1, 1CO 9:5; 1TH 4:17

Jesus Christ's lordship is the basis for his relationship with Christians

It is a personal relationship

PHP 3:8 See also ACT 16:15; ROM 5:11, ROM 14:8; 1CO 6:17; 2PE 2:20

``The name of the Lord'' expresses this relationship

1CO 6:11 See also ACT 8:16, ACT 15:26, ACT 19:5, ACT 21:13; EPH 5:20; COL 3:17

``Jesus our Lord'' expresses this relationship

EPH 6:24 See also 1CO 15:57; 2CO 8:9; GAL 6:14; EPH 5:20; 1TH 5:28; 2TI 1:8

``In the Lord'' as an expression of this relationship

PHM 1:16 See also ROM 16:12; EPH 6:10; PHP 1:14, PHP 2:29, PHP 3:1; PHM 1:20

The ``grace of the Lord'' as God's provision for this relationship

1TI 1:14 See also 2CO 13:14; 2TH 3:18; PHM 1:25; 2PE 3:18; JUD 1:21

Jesus Christ's lordship is the basis for Christian obedience

COL 2:6 See also ROM 12:11; COL 3:23; 2TH 1:8; 2TI 2:19; 1PE 3:15

Jesus Christ's lordship will be fully revealed

1TH 4:16 See also 1CO 1:7; PHP 3:20; 1TH 5:23; 2TH 1:7; 1TI 6:14

Jesus Christ's lordship signifies his absolute authority

Jesus Christ as judge of all

1CO 4:4 See also 1CO 11:32; 2CO 10:18; 1TH 4:6; 2TH 1:8–1:9

Jesus Christ as Lord of all

ROM 10:12 See also ACT 10:36; 1CO 8:6; PHP 2:10–2:11; COL 1:16–1:17

title: 2227: Immanuel
content:
An OT name given to Jesus Christ, meaning “God with us”.

Actual uses of the name Immanuel

ISA 7:14 The name was given originally to a child of Isaiah's day who would signify God's salvation and judgment for Judah; ISA 8:7–8:10 The original fulfilment of the Immanuel prophecy would bring judgment on Judah; MAT 1:22–1:23 Isaiah's prophecy is fulfilled through the virginal conception of Jesus Christ.

The significance of Immanuel

God is with his people

GEN 28:15 See also EXO 33:14; NUM 14:9; DEU 4:7; 2CH 13:12; PSA 46:7, PSA 46:11; HAG 2:4; ROM 8:31; HEB 13:5

God dwells with his people

REV 21:3 See also LEV 26:11–26:12; EZK 37:27; 2CO 6:16

Jesus Christ is always with believers

MAT 28:20 See also JHN 14:18, JHN 14:23, JHN 15:4–15:5, JHN 15:7; ACT 18:10; GAL 2:20

The Holy Spirit is always with believers

JHN 14:17 See also ROM 8:9–8:11; 1CO 3:16; 2CO 1:22; EPH 2:22; 2TI 1:14; 1JN 4:13

resource.label: 2203: Jesus Christ, titles and names of
7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 2230: Messiah, coming of
content:
The coming of a figure chosen and anointed by God to deliver and redeem his people. Anointing was seen as a sign of being chosen by God for a special task of leadership or responsibility. The OT looked ahead to the final coming of such a figure to usher in a new era in the history of the people of God; the NT sees this expectation fulfilled in the person and work of Jesus Christ.

Anointing as a sign of being chosen by God

Anointing with oil

LEV 8:12 See also 1SA 16:12–16:13; 2SA 2:4, 2SA 5:3 parallel 1CH 11:3; 1KI 1:39; 2KI 9:6; PSA 89:20

Anointing with the Holy Spirit

ISA 61:1 See also LUK 4:18; JDG 14:19; 1SA 11:6, 1SA 16:13; ISA 11:2; MAT 12:18; ISA 42:1

Anointing as a sign of national and spiritual leadership

Anointed to be king

1CH 29:22 See also JDG 9:7–9:15; 1SA 9:16, 1SA 15:17; 2SA 19:10; 1KI 1:34; 2KI 9:3, 2KI 11:12 parallel 2CH 23:11; 2CH 22:7

Anointed to be priest

EXO 40:13–40:15 See also EXO 28:41, EXO 29:7; LEV 7:35–7:36, LEV 16:32; NUM 3:3

Anointed to be judge

The coming of the Spirit on some of the judges is a spiritual anointing: JDG 3:10, JDG 6:34, JDG 11:29, JDG 15:14

Anointed to be prophet

1KI 19:16; ; ISA 48:16; ; EZK 11:5, EZK 37:1; ; MIC 3:8

The expectation of a future Messiah

The future anointed king

GEN 49:10; ISA 16:5 See also NUM 24:17; 2SA 7:12–7:14 parallel 1CH 17:11–17:13; PSA 2:7–2:9, PSA 45:6–45:7, PSA 110:1–110:2, PSA 132:11–132:12; ISA 9:6–9:7; EZK 37:24; MIC 5:2; ZEC 9:9

The future anointed priest

PSA 110:4 See also ZEC 6:13

The future anointed prophet

ISA 61:1–61:2 See also DEU 18:18

The future anointed judge

ISA 2:4, ISA 11:3–11:4; ; MIC 4:3

The future anointed servant of God

ISA 42:1–42:4 See also ISA 49:1–49:6, ISA 50:4–50:9, ISA 52:13–53:12

title: 2233: Son of Man
content:
A Hebrew and Aramaic term, often used to mean a human being in general. In the Gospels Jesus Christ uses it to refer to himself in his earthly ministry and his future death, exaltation and coming as judge and Saviour.

Sons of men as a term for humanity in general

Human beings are inferior to God

PSA 115:16 The Hebrew term here translated “man” literally means “sons of men”. Many of the OT references cited in this theme follow this pattern. See also PSA 11:4, PSA 33:13–33:14, PSA 45:2

Human beings are dependent on God's care

PSA 8:4 See also PSA 36:7, PSA 80:17, PSA 107:8, PSA 107:15, PSA 107:21, PSA 107:31

Human beings are mortal

ECC 9:3 See also ECC 3:18–3:19; EZK 31:14

Human beings are sinful and untrustworthy

PSA 146:3 See also PSA 14:2–14:3 parallel PSA 53:2–53:3; MIC 5:7

Son of Man as a term for individual men

The sons of Adam

GEN 4:1–4:2 “a man” is literally “son of Adam” in Hebrew; ; GEN 5:3–5:4

Ezekiel

EZK 2:1 Ezekiel is called “son of man” 93 times, though this form of address is unique to him in the OT. It may emphasise his remoteness or his humanity. See also EZK 2:3, EZK 2:6, EZK 2:8, EZK 3:1

Other people

JOB 25:6; ; DAN 8:17

A son of man as a ruler of God's future kingdom

DAN 7:13 The Aramaic “one like a son of man” stresses this person's humanity and mysterious identity; DAN 7:13–7:14 He enters God's presence and is given final authority over God's kingdom; DAN 7:27 He shares the kingdom with God's people. See also DAN 7:18 “saints of the Most High” have sometimes been interpreted as angels

Son of Man as a title for Jesus Christ

Used by Jesus Christ to emphasise his humanity and authority

MRK 2:10–2:11 parallels MAT 9:6, LUK 5:24MRK 8:31 parallel LUK 9:22

The Son of Man receives God's kingdom

ACT 7:56 See also DAN 7:13; MRK 14:62 parallels MAT 26:64, LUK 22:69; HEB 2:6–2:9; PSA 8:4–8:6

title: 2300: Jesus Christ, ministry and work of
content:
• 2303: Jesus Christ, as creator
  • 2375: kingdom of God

title: 2303: Jesus Christ, as creator
content:
Scripture identifies the pre-existent Jesus Christ as involved in the work of creation, and relates this to his work in redemption, by which a new creation is brought out of the ruins of the old.

Jesus Christ's creation of the present world

Jesus Christ created all things

JHN 1:3 See also JHN 1:10; ACT 3:15; 1CO 8:6; COL 1:15–1:16; HEB 1:2

Jesus Christ sustains the created universe

HEB 1:3 See also 1CO 8:6; COL 1:17; REV 3:14 The word translated “ruler” can mean either “beginning” or “first in rank”.

Jesus Christ will bring the entire work of creation to perfection

EPH 1:9–1:10 See also ROM 8:19–8:22; COL 1:20

Jesus Christ makes a new creation possible

Jesus Christ recreates people through a new birth

2CO 5:17 See also JHN 1:12–1:13, JHN 3:5–3:6; GAL 6:15; JAS 1:18

Jesus Christ's work of new creation should be evident in believers' lives

EPH 4:24 See also EPH 3:15; COL 3:10

Through Jesus Christ a new heaven and earth will be created

2PE 3:13 The promise was given in Isa 65:17; 66:22. See also REV 21:1, REV 21:4–21:5

resource.label: 2342: Jesus Christ, Holy Spirit in life of
2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of

title: 2306: Jesus Christ, as high priest
content:
Jesus Christ, being a truly human high priest, perfectly represents humanity before God. He made atonement for sins by his own sacrificial death. Being a truly divine high priest, this act of Christ's was perfect, once for all and of eternal value.

The ministry of Jesus Christ as high priest

He made atonement for sin

HEB 2:17 See also HEB 8:3, HEB 9:7, HEB 9:11–9:12, HEB 13:11–13:13

He represents human beings before God

HEB 5:1 See also ROM 8:34; 1JN 2:1–2:2

He entered the Most Holy Place

HEB 9:24 On the Day of Atonement the high priest entered the Most Holy Place (the sanctuary) which represented the presence of God. Jesus Christ fulfilled this ritual by entering heaven. See also HEB 4:14, HEB 6:19–6:20, HEB 9:7, HEB 9:12

He makes believers perfect

HEB 10:14 See also HEB 10:10, HEB 13:12

He brings believers close to God

HEB 10:19–10:22

He helps those being tempted

HEB 2:18 See also HEB 4:15, HEB 5:2

He intercedes continually

HEB 7:24–7:25 See also JHN 17:20; ROM 8:34

Jesus Christ, the human high priest

As a man he is able to represent human beings

HEB 2:17 See also HEB 5:1

He is divinely appointed

HEB 5:5–5:6 See also HEB 3:1–3:2

Jesus Christ, the divine high priest

He is sinless

HEB 7:26–7:27 Jesus Christ is strongly contrasted with other high priests because he did not have to offer sacrifices for his own sins.

He is eternal

HEB 6:20 See also HEB 5:6, HEB 7:17, HEB 7:21, HEB 7:28

His atoning work was completed once for all

HEB 7:27 Other high priests had to repeat their sacrifices. See also HEB 9:12, HEB 9:25–9:26, HEB 9:28, HEB 10:10

He is exalted

HEB 8:1 See also ZEC 6:13; HEB 7:26

Jesus Christ's high priesthood was of the order of Melchizedek

HEB 6:20 The priesthood of Melchizedek resembles that of Jesus Christ because he too was king and had neither beginning nor end. See also PSA 110:4; HEB 5:6, HEB 5:10, HEB 7:1–7:4, HEB 7:14–7:17

Responses to Jesus Christ's high priesthood

HEB 4:14–4:15 a call to keep to the faith; ; HEB 4:16 an invitation to seek God's help in times of need; ; HEB 10:19–10:22 an invitation to draw near to God; ; HEB 12:1–12:3 a call to run the race of faith; ; HEB 12:28–12:29 a call to offer thanksgiving and worship

title: 2309: Jesus Christ, as judge
content:
Jesus Christ executed judgment against the forces of evil through his death on the cross. Individuals will be judged according to their response to his saving grace.

Jesus Christ is appointed judge by God the Father

JHN 5:22 See also JHN 5:27; ACT 10:42, ACT 17:31

Jesus Christ is a just judge

JHN 5:30 See also ISA 11:3–11:4; MIC 4:3; JHN 8:15–8:16; ACT 17:31; REV 19:11

Jesus Christ's death brought judgment on Satan and evil world powers

JHN 12:31–12:33 See also JHN 16:11

Jesus Christ will act as judge at the end of time

2TI 4:1 See also MAT 25:31–25:32; ROM 2:16; 2TH 1:7–1:10; 2TI 4:8; REV 19:11–19:16, REV 22:12

People will be judged according to their response to Jesus Christ

JHN 3:18 See also MAT 10:32–10:33 parallel LUK 12:8–12:9; MRK 8:38 parallel LUK 9:26; JHN 9:39, JHN 12:48

Jesus Christ's teaching on judgment

Jesus Christ will separate the righteous from the unrighteous

MAT 25:31–25:32 See also MAT 3:12, MAT 13:24–13:30, MAT 13:36–13:42 the parable of the weeds; MAT 13:47–13:50 the parable of the net; MAT 25:33–25:46 the parable of the sheep and the goats

Those who reject Jesus Christ will be punished

DAN 2:34–2:35; ; MAT 21:33–21:44 parallels MRK 12:1–12:11, LUK 20:9–20:18 the parable of the tenants

Christians will stand before Jesus Christ as judge and be rewarded for what they have done

MAT 16:27 See also MAT 25:14–25:30 the parable of the talents; LUK 19:12–19:27 the parable of the ten minas; 2CO 5:10

The day of Jesus Christ's return as judge is unknown

MRK 13:32–13:36 See also MAT 24:36–24:44 parallel LUK 17:26–17:37; MAT 25:1–25:13 the parable of the ten virgins

resource.label: 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of

title: 2312: Jesus Christ, as king
content:
Jesus Christ is declared king at his birth: he descends from the royal line of David. People rejected his kingly claims at his crucifixion but God exalted him to his rightful place of power and majesty. At the end of time Jesus Christ will rule the nations for ever.

Jesus Christ descends from the royal line of David

He was the Son of David

MAT 1:1 The Messiah was expected to come from David's line. The genealogy given by Matthew follows the royal line of the kings descended from David. See also MAT 9:27, MAT 12:23, MAT 15:22; LUK 1:32–1:33, LUK 2:4, LUK 3:31

He was born in Bethlehem, the town of David

JHN 7:42 See also MAT 2:3–2:6; MIC 5:2; LUK 2:15–2:16

Jesus Christ is regarded as king

JHN 1:49 By Nathanael. See also MAT 2:1–2:2 by the Magi; JHN 6:15 by the crowds; ACT 17:7 by Jason of Thessalonica

Jesus Christ refused worldly kingdoms

JHN 18:36 See also MAT 4:8–4:10 parallel LUK 4:5–4:8; JHN 6:15

Jesus Christ entered Jerusalem as a king

MAT 21:1–21:9 parallel JHN 12:12–12:15 See also ZEC 9:9

Jesus Christ's kingship is an issue at his trial

Pilate questions Jesus Christ

LUK 23:2–23:3 parallels MAT 27:11, MRK 15:2, JHN 18:33 Pilate's first words to Jesus Christ are the same in all the Gospels.

Jesus Christ claims to be a spiritual king

JHN 18:36–18:37 See also MAT 27:11 parallels MRK 15:2, LUK 23:3

Pilate appeals to the crowd to accept Jesus Christ as their king

MRK 15:9

The crowd reject Jesus Christ as king

MRK 15:12–15:13 See also JHN 19:12–19:15

Jesus Christ is mocked as king

MAT 27:27–27:30 parallels MRK 15:16–15:20, JHN 19:2–19:3

Jesus Christ is declared king at his crucifixion

JHN 19:19–19:22 parallels MAT 27:37, MRK 15:26, LUK 23:38 Jesus Christ's claim to be king was the “crime” for which he was executed.

God has exalted Jesus Christ to his rightful place as king

HEB 1:3 See also HEB 8:1, HEB 12:2; REV 5:6, REV 7:17, REV 22:1–22:3

As king, Jesus Christ welcomes believers into his kingdom

LUK 23:42–23:43 See also 2TI 4:18; REV 3:20–3:21

Jesus Christ is king of all kings

He will rule over the nations

REV 1:5 See also PSA 2:7–2:9; DAN 7:13–7:14; MAT 19:28, MAT 25:31–25:32; ROM 15:12; 1CO 15:25; PHP 2:9–2:10; REV 12:5, REV 17:14, REV 19:11–19:16

He is king for ever

REV 11:15 See also ISA 9:7; LUK 1:33; HEB 1:8; PSA 45:6

title: 2315: Jesus Christ, as Lamb
content:
Jesus Christ is referred to as the “Lamb of God”. This symbol points to Christ being a perfect sacrifice for sin. It also conveys his meekness and his willingness to submit to suffering and death.

The imagery of the lamb

An image of innocence and attraction

ISA 11:6 See also LEV 23:12; 2SA 12:3

An image of submissiveness and vulnerability

JER 11:19 See also ISA 40:11; LUK 10:3; JHN 21:15

An image of quiet suffering

ISA 53:7

An image of sacrifice

EXO 29:38 “each day” here refers to the seven-day period of the consecration of priests. See also EXO 12:27; LEV 3:7, LEV 4:32, LEV 14:13; EZK 46:13; MRK 14:12 parallel LUK 22:7

Jesus Christ is likened to a lamb

JHN 1:36 See also JHN 1:29; 1CO 5:7; 1PE 1:19; REV 5:6

The symbolism of the lamb applies to Jesus Christ

Jesus Christ is innocent and draws people to himself

LUK 23:41 See also JHN 12:32; HEB 4:15, HEB 7:26; 1PE 2:22; ISA 53:9

Jesus Christ was submissive and vulnerable

MAT 26:38–26:39 parallels MRK 14:35–14:36, LUK 22:41–22:42 See also JHN 3:27; 1CO 1:27; PHP 2:7–2:8; HEB 4:15

Jesus Christ suffered quietly

MAT 27:28–27:31 parallel MRK 15:17–15:20 See also MAT 26:62–26:63 parallel MRK 14:60–14:61; MAT 27:12–27:14 parallel MRK 15:3–15:5; LUK 23:8–23:11; JHN 19:2–19:3; ACT 8:32–8:35; ISA 53:7–53:8; 1PE 2:23

Jesus Christ is seen as a sacrifice

1PE 1:18–1:19 See also GEN 22:8; EXO 13:13; ISA 53:4–53:6, ISA 53:10–53:11; JHN 1:29; 1CO 5:7; REV 12:11, REV 13:8

Jesus Christ as the glorious Lamb in the book of Revelation

The Lamb is worthy

REV 5:8–5:12 See also REV 7:9–7:10, REV 15:3

The Lamb is upon the throne

REV 17:14 See also REV 5:6, REV 7:17, REV 22:1–22:3

The Lamb will execute judgment

REV 6:16 See also REV 6:1–6:3, REV 14:10

The Lamb is the bridegroom of the church

REV 19:7–19:9 See also REV 21:9

title: 2318: Jesus Christ, as prophet
content:
Jesus Christ was acclaimed as a prophet by those who witnessed his miracles and heard his teaching. They recognised him as a bearer of the word of God, who spoke with authority concerning the nature and purposes of God.

Examples of those who recognised Jesus Christ as a prophet

People anticipating the coming prophet

JHN 6:14 The reference is to the promised prophet, who, like Moses, would see God face to face (see Dt 18:15,18). See also JHN 1:21, JHN 7:40, JHN 7:52

A Samaritan woman

JHN 4:17–4:19

Witnesses of Jesus Christ's miracles

MAT 14:1–14:5 parallel MRK 6:14–6:15; ; MAT 16:13–16:14 parallels MRK 8:27–8:28, LUK 9:18–9:19

The crowd as Jesus Christ approached Jerusalem

MAT 21:10–21:11, MAT 21:46

Two of Jesus Christ's disciples after his death

LUK 24:19

The apostles Peter and Stephen

ACT 3:20–3:23, ACT 7:37; ; DEU 18:15–18:18

Indications of Jesus Christ being a prophet

He announced coming blessing from God

JHN 7:37–7:40 See also MAT 5:3–5:12 parallel LUK 6:20–6:23; MAT 13:16–13:17 parallel LUK 10:23–10:24; JHN 20:19–20:21, JHN 20:29

He announced coming judgment and woe

ACT 3:23 See also MAT 11:20–11:24 parallel LUK 10:13–10:15; MAT 23:13–23:39; LUK 6:24–6:26

He possessed supernatural knowledge and insight

title: 2357: Jesus Christ, parables of
content:
A central feature of Jesus Christ's teaching was the use of extended similies and short stories about the kingdom of God, based on human experience. Christ used them frequently to call for a response to his message.

Parables are a central feature of Jesus Christ's teaching

MAT 13:34–13:35 See also PSA 78:2; MRK 4:33–4:34

Different characteristics of Jesus Christ's parables

They vary in complexity and length

MAT 7:6, MAT 13:18–13:23 parallels MRK 4:13–4:20, LUK 8:11–8:15; ; MAT 13:37–13:45, MAT 13:52

They vary in their use of metaphors, word pictures, objects and actions

MAT 5:13–5:16, MAT 13:44, MAT 18:2–18:3, MAT 21:18–21:22 parallels MRK 11:12–11:14, MRK 11:20–11:24; ; LUK 15:8–15:10

They have different levels of meaning

Often the full impact of a parable may be lost if the Jewish context is not understood: LUK 10:30–10:37, LUK 15:11–15:32

They draw on human experience

MAT 13:33 parallel LUK 13:20–13:21; ; MAT 18:12–18:14 parallel LUK 15:4–15:7; ; LUK 7:41–7:43

The themes of Jesus Christ's parables

The kingdom of God

MRK 4:30 parallel LUK 13:18 See also MAT 13:31–13:32 parallels MRK 4:31–4:32, LUK 13:19–13:21; MAT 13:44–13:45; MRK 4:26–4:29

LUK 15:20 See also MAT 7:9–7:11 parallel LUK 11:11–11:13; MAT 18:12–18:14 parallel LUK 15:4–15:7; LUK 18:7–18:8

Right behaviour

LUK 8:9–8:10 parallels MAT 13:11–13:16, MRK 4:11–4:12 The meaning of Jesus Christ's parables was concealed only from those whose hearts and minds were closed. See also ISA 6:9–6:10

title: 2360: Jesus Christ, prayers of
content:
Prayer was the essence of Jesus Christ's relationship with the Father. He prayed for himself and his mission, and he continues to pray for all believers.

Jesus Christ's practice of prayer

He prayed regularly

LUK 5:16 Luke records more instances of Jesus Christ praying than any other Gospel writer.

He often prayed alone

MRK 1:35 See also MAT 14:23 parallel MRK 6:46; LUK 6:12, LUK 9:18

Prayer at specific times in Jesus Christ's life

Before his death: MAT 26:36–26:46 parallels MRK 14:32–14:41, LUK 22:39–22:46; ; JHN 17:1–17:26; MAT 27:46 parallel MRK 15:34; ; LUK 23:34, LUK 23:46, LUK 3:21–3:22 at his baptism; ; LUK 6:12–6:13 before choosing the apostles; ; LUK 9:28–9:29 before his transfiguration

Characteristics of Jesus Christ's prayers

Communion with his Father

MAT 6:9 parallel LUK 11:2; ; MAT 11:27 parallel LUK 10:22

Submission to his Father

HEB 5:7 See also MAT 6:10, MAT 26:36 parallels MRK 14:36, LUK 22:42

Giving praise and thanks to his Father

MAT 11:25–11:26 parallel LUK 10:21 See also MAT 14:19 parallels MRK 6:41, LUK 9:16, JHN 6:11; MAT 15:36 parallel MRK 8:6–8:7; MAT 26:26–26:27 parallels MRK 14:22–14:23, LUK 22:17–22:19, 1CO 11:24 the Lord's Supper; LUK 24:30; JHN 11:41–11:42

The scope of Jesus Christ's prayers

Jesus Christ's continuing ministry of prayer

HEB 7:25 See also ROM 8:34; 1JN 2:1

Jesus Christ's teaching about prayer

MAT 6:9–6:15 parallel LUK 11:2–11:4 See also MAT 5:44, MAT 6:5–6:8; LUK 6:28, LUK 18:1–18:8, LUK 18:9–18:14, LUK 21:36

title: 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of
content:

Jesus Christ's mission as preaching and teaching

Jesus Christ was regarded as a teacher and prophet

The sources of Jesus Christ's preaching and teaching

Jesus Christ's words were grounded in Scripture

JHN 7:16 See also JHN 3:2, JHN 8:28, JHN 12:49–12:50

The kingdom of God

LUK 9:11 See also MAT 4:17, MAT 4:23, MAT 6:33, MAT 13:24; MRK 1:15; JHN 3:3

God as Father

Jesus Christ invited a response

MAT 11:28–11:30 See also MAT 13:23 parallels MRK 4:20, LUK 8:15; MAT 22:8–22:10; LUK 14:21–14:24; JHN 5:24

Jesus Christ looked for an obedient response

LUK 11:28 See also MAT 7:24–7:27, MAT 11:15, MAT 13:23 parallels MRK 4:20, LUK 8:15; MAT 28:20; MRK 4:9; JHN 14:23–14:24

It had authority

Jesus Christ lived out what he preached and taught

JHN 10:38 The “miracles” (literally “works”) conveyed the same message as Jesus Christ's words. See also MAT 11:29, MAT 16:24 parallels MRK 8:34, LUK 9:23; JHN 13:15, JHN 13:34

Jesus Christ's preaching and teaching methods

His use of lessons drawn from people's experience

MAT 9:16–9:17 parallels MRK 2:21–2:22, LUK 5:36–5:37; ; MAT 12:11–12:12, MAT 18:12 parallel LUK 15:4; ; LUK 9:62, LUK 13:15–13:16

His use of parables

His use of everyday objects

His use of questions

MAT 6:25–6:28, MAT 21:24–21:25 parallels MRK 11:29, LUK 20:3–20:4; ; LUK 10:36–10:37

title: 2366: Jesus Christ, prophecies concerning
content:
The OT points ahead to the person and ministry of Jesus Christ. Christ's fulfilment of OT prophecy is often noted by NT writers as a demonstration of God's faithfulness to his promises of salvation and as confirmation of the divine authority of Jesus Christ.

Jesus Christ fulfils OT prophecy

He fulfils the OT as a whole

LUK 24:27 See also MAT 5:17; LUK 24:44; 2CO 1:20

He fulfils God's promises to Abraham

GAL 3:14 See also LUK 1:54–1:55, LUK 1:72–1:74; GAL 3:16

He fulfils God's promises through the prophets

ACT 10:43 See also MAT 26:56; ACT 3:18, ACT 3:24, ACT 13:27; ROM 1:2; 1PE 1:10

Prophecies about Jesus Christ's birth

MAT 1:23 See also ISA 7:14; MAT 1:21; LUK 1:31–1:32; ISA 9:6; MAT 2:6–2:7; MIC 5:2; MAT 2:15; HOS 11:1

Prophecies about Jesus Christ's ministry

He brings good news

LUK 4:17–4:19 See also ISA 61:1–61:2; MAT 4:13–4:16; ISA 9:1–9:2

He divides people and is rejected

LUK 2:34–2:35 See also MAT 3:11–3:12 parallels MRK 1:7–1:8, LUK 3:16–3:17; MAT 21:42 parallels MRK 12:10–12:11, LUK 20:17; PSA 118:22–118:23; JHN 13:18; PSA 41:9

He teaches in parables

MAT 13:35 See also PSA 78:2; MAT 13:13–13:15 parallels MRK 4:11–4:12, LUK 8:10; ISA 6:9–6:10

The Lord's Supper

MAT 26:28 parallels MRK 14:24, LUK 22:20 See also ISA 53:12; JER 31:34

The betrayal

MAT 27:9–27:10 JER 19:1–19:13; ; ZEC 11:12–11:13; JHN 18:9, JHN 6:39

The cross

MAT 27:46 parallel MRK 15:34 See also PSA 22:1; MAT 27:35; PSA 22:18; LUK 23:46; PSA 31:5

Prophecies about Jesus Christ's resurrection and ascension

MAT 12:39–12:40 parallel LUK 11:29–11:30 See also JON 1:17; ACT 2:25–2:28; PSA 16:8–16:11; ACT 2:31, ACT 13:35, ACT 2:34; PSA 110:1; EPH 4:8; PSA 68:18

Prophecies about Jesus Christ's titles

Jesus Christ as Son of God

ACT 13:32–13:33 See also PSA 2:7; HEB 1:5, HEB 5:5; MAT 22:44 parallels MRK 12:36, LUK 20:42; PSA 110:1; HEB 1:8–1:9; PSA 45:6–45:7

Jesus Christ as Son of Man

MAT 26:64 parallels MRK 14:62, LUK 22:69 See also DAN 7:13–7:14; MRK 8:31 parallel LUK 9:22

REV 22:16 See also 2SA 7:12–7:16 parallel 1CH 17:11–17:14; LUK 1:32–1:33; ISA 9:7; ACT 13:34; ISA 55:3; ROM 15:12; ISA 11:1

Jesus Christ as the coming Messianic king

MAT 26:31 parallel MRK 14:27; ; ZEC 13:7; ; MAT 21:4–21:5; ; ZEC 9:9

Jesus Christ as the suffering servant

ACT 8:32–8:35 See also ISA 53:7–53:8; MAT 12:17–12:21; ISA 42:1–42:4

Jesus Christ as the prophet who is to come

ACT 3:22 See also DEU 18:18; LUK 4:18–4:19; ISA 61:1–61:2; JHN 6:14

Jesus Christ as priest

HEB 7:21 See also PSA 110:4; HEB 5:5–5:6, HEB 7:17

title: 2369: Jesus Christ, responses to
content:
The NT records a number of responses to Jesus Christ on the part of those who encountered him, both positive and negative.

Negative responses to Jesus Christ

Rejection

MAT 21:42; ; MRK 7:5–7:8; ; LUK 4:28–4:30, LUK 10:16

Lack of faith

MRK 6:4–6:6 parallel MAT 13:57–13:58

Questioning his authority

See also MRK 11:27–11:33 parallels MAT 21:23–21:27, LUK 20:1–20:8

Hardening of hearts

MAT 13:10–13:17; ; JHN 12:37–12:41

Attributing his power to satanic forces

MAT 9:33–9:34

Demanding his death

See also LUK 23:20–23:24; JHN 10:22–10:40

As Messiah

JHN 1:41 See also LUK 2:11, LUK 9:20; ACT 5:42

As King

As Lord

JHN 6:68 See also JHN 13:13

As Son of David

MAT 15:22

As Son of God

As Teacher

MAT 19:16 parallels MRK 10:17, LUK 18:18 See also MAT 23:10; JHN 11:28

LUK 17:11–17:19

title: 2372: Jesus Christ, victory of
content:
Jesus Christ triumphs over and disarms all the powers of evil arrayed against him. Believers are able to share in this victory through faith in Jesus Christ.

The scope of Jesus Christ's victory

Over temptation

HEB 4:15 See also MAT 4:1–4:11 parallel LUK 4:1–4:13

Over sin

ROM 5:20–5:21 See also 1JN 3:5, 1JN 3:8

Over Satan

JHN 12:31 Satan is called “the prince of this world” in John's Gospel. See also LUK 10:18–10:19; JHN 14:30, JHN 16:11; HEB 2:14–2:15

Over evil spirits

MRK 1:23–1:27 parallel LUK 4:33–4:36

Over all powers

COL 2:15 “powers and authorities” include evil spiritual beings. See also ROM 8:38–8:39; 1CO 15:24–15:25; EPH 1:21–1:22, EPH 4:8; PSA 68:18

Over the world

JHN 16:33 Here, as elsewhere in John's Gospel, “world” refers to the human race in rebellion against God. See also 2TH 2:8 “the lawless one” represents the leader of human forces of evil; 1JN 5:4–5:5; REV 17:12–17:14, REV 19:11–19:21

Over death

ROM 6:9 See also ACT 2:24; 1CO 15:26, 1CO 15:54–15:57; 2TI 1:10; REV 1:18

The manner of Jesus Christ's victory

By the written word of God

MAT 4:4–4:10 parallel LUK 4:4–4:12

By his death on the cross

HEB 2:14 See also COL 2:15; REV 12:11

By God's authority

LUK 11:20 parallel MAT 12:28

By his spoken word

MAT 8:16 See also MAT 8:8 parallel LUK 7:7; MAT 8:13

Consequences of Jesus Christ's victory

Assurance for believers

JHN 16:33; ; 1TH 4:13; ; REV 5:5

Victory for believers

1CO 15:57 See also ROM 7:21–7:25, ROM 8:37, ROM 16:20

title: 2375: kingdom of God
content: Or, less frequently, “kingdom of heaven”, the kingly rule of God in the lives of people and nations. It refers to the recognition of the authority of God, rather than a definite geographical area, and begins with the ministry of Jesus Christ.

title: 2376: kingdom of God, coming of
content:
The kingdom of God comes into being wherever the kingly authority of God is acknowledged. Although God is always sovereign, Scripture looks to a future “realm” or “reign” of salvation. This has come in Christ and yet will come in its fulness only when Jesus Christ returns.

God is sovereign over Israel and over the whole earth

PSA 47:7–47:8 See also EXO 15:18; 1SA 12:12; 1CH 16:31, 1CH 28:5, 1CH 29:11–29:12; PSA 9:7–9:8, PSA 45:6, PSA 93:1–93:2, PSA 103:19, PSA 145:11–145:13; ISA 37:16; DAN 4:34–4:35

The coming reign of God

Its expectation

ISA 51:4–51:5; MRK 15:43 parallel LUK 23:51 See also ISA 2:2–2:4 parallel MIC 4:1–4:3; ISA 32:1; JER 3:17; DAN 2:44, DAN 7:18, DAN 7:21–7:22, DAN 7:27; ZEC 8:22, ZEC 14:9; MRK 11:10

Its assocication with the coming of the Messiah

ISA 9:6–9:7; DAN 7:14 “Son of Man” was Jesus Christ's usual way of referring to himself. See also ISA 11:1–11:9; JER 23:5–23:6; MIC 5:2

The kingdom of God was central in the preaching of Jesus Christ and the apostles

MAT 24:14; LUK 8:1; ACT 28:31 See also MAT 4:17, MAT 4:23, MAT 9:35, MAT 10:7 Jesus Christ's instructions to the Twelve; MRK 1:13–1:14; LUK 4:43, LUK 9:2, LUK 9:11, LUK 10:9; ACT 1:3, ACT 1:6–1:8, ACT 8:12, ACT 19:8, ACT 20:25, ACT 28:23

The kingdom of God has come in Christ: it is present

MAT 11:12 Following the Jewish convention of avoiding the use of the divine name, Matthew usually speaks of “the kingdom of heaven”. See also MAT 3:1–3:2, MAT 4:17, MAT 13:31–13:32 parallels MRK 4:30–4:32, LUK 13:18–13:19; MAT 13:33 parallel LUK 13:20–13:21; MAT 16:28 parallels MRK 9:1, LUK 9:27; LUK 11:20, LUK 16:16, LUK 17:20–17:21

The kingdom of God will come in its fulness only when Jesus Christ returns: it is future

LUK 22:18 parallels MAT 26:29, MRK 14:25 See also MAT 6:10 parallel LUK 11:2; MAT 25:31, MAT 25:34; LUK 22:16; 1CO 15:24; 2TI 4:18; REV 11:15, REV 12:10

title: 2377: kingdom of God, entry into
content:
Entering or inheriting the kingdom of God is the privilege of those who acknowledge and live by the rule of God and have become part of the new order of salvation and righteousness in Christ.

Entry into the kingdom of God is of vital importance

It is costly

MAT 13:44; ACT 14:22 See also MAT 8:19–8:20 parallel LUK 9:57–9:58; MAT 13:45–13:46; LUK 18:29–18:30; 2TH 1:5; REV 1:9

It is a matter of urgency

LUK 9:59–9:62 parallel MAT 8:21–8:22

Conditions of entry into the kingdom of God

Childlike trust

MRK 10:15 parallel LUK 18:17 See also MAT 18:3

To be born again of God's Spirit

JHN 3:3 See also JHN 3:5; 1CO 15:50

Obedience to God's will

MAT 7:21

Warnings about entry into the kingdom of God

The way is narrow

LUK 13:24–13:28 See also MAT 7:13–7:14, MAT 23:13

The wicked will not inherit the kingdom

1CO 6:9–6:10 See also MAT 5:20; MRK 9:43–9:47; GAL 5:19–5:21; EPH 5:5

The need for readiness and watchfulness

MAT 24:42–24:44 See also MAT 24:37–24:39 parallel LUK 17:26–17:27; MAT 25:13; LUK 12:35–12:40

Entry is not based on outward appearances nor granted to all who claim to know the Lord

MAT 7:21–7:23 See also MAT 13:24–13:30, MAT 13:47–13:50; LUK 13:25–13:27

The kingdom of God is a kingdom of grace

It belongs to those qualified by God

COL 1:12–1:13 See also LUK 12:32, LUK 22:29–22:30

It belongs to the poor and the poor in spirit

LUK 6:20 parallel MAT 5:3 See also MAT 11:5 parallel LUK 7:22; MAT 19:23–19:24 parallels MRK 10:23–10:25, LUK 18:24–18:25 It is hard for the rich to enter the kingdom of God; JAS 2:5, JAS 5:1

It belongs to the childlike

MAT 19:14 parallels MRK 10:14, LUK 18:16

It belongs to sinners

MRK 2:17 parallels MAT 9:12–9:13, LUK 5:31–5:32 See also MAT 21:31

It belongs to those who are persecuted for Jesus Christ's sake

MAT 5:10 parallel LUK 6:22–6:23

It belongs to Gentiles as well as to Jews

MAT 8:11 See also MAT 21:43, MAT 22:8–22:10 those initially uninvited represent the Gentiles; LUK 14:21–14:24

title: 2378: kingdom of God, characteristics of
content:
Those who have entered the kingdom must live according to its values, anticipating the reign of peace which will come when Jesus Christ returns.

The kingdom of God does not conform to the standards of this world

JHN 18:36; ROM 14:17

Those who inherit the kingdom of God are to bear its fruit

1TH 2:12 See also MAT 25:34–25:36; 2PE 1:10–1:11

The kingdom of God is and will be a kingdom of peace

Peace between people

ISA 2:2–2:4 parallel MIC 4:1–4:4JAS 3:18 See also ISA 9:5, ISA 19:24–19:25; MIC 5:4–5:5; MAT 5:9 The Sermon on the Mount (Mt 5:1–7:29) is often thought of as a description of life in the kingdom.

The peace and prosperity of all creation

ISA 11:6–11:9 See also ISA 35:1–35:2, ISA 35:9, ISA 41:17–41:19; EZK 47:9, EZK 47:12; HOS 2:21–2:22

The kingdom of God is a kingdom of forgiveness

MAT 6:12 parallel LUK 11:4; ; MAT 18:21–18:35; ; LUK 17:3–17:4

Status in the kingdom of God

title: 2400: Jesus Christ, gospel of
content:

title: 2410: cross, the
content: An instrument of execution, used especially by the Roman authorities for putting criminals to death. The sufferings of Jesus Christ on the cross, foreshadowed in the OT, are related in the NT gospel accounts. Scripture sees the death of Christ as central to the Christian faith. Through the cross and resurrection of Christ, God achieved the redemption of believers.

title: 2411: cross, predictions of
content:
The death of Jesus Christ fulfils OT predictions and was clearly anticipated in his teaching.

The cross foreshadowed in the OT

PSA 22:1 See also GEN 3:15; PSA 22:6–22:7, PSA 22:16–22:18, PSA 31:5, PSA 69:21; ISA 52:12–52:13; ZEC 12:10, ZEC 13:7

Jesus Christ predicts his death on the cross

Jesus Christ's first announcement of his death

MAT 16:21 parallels MRK 8:31, LUK 9:22

Jesus Christ's second announcement of his death

MAT 17:22–17:23 See also MRK 9:31

Jesus Christ's third announcement of his death

MAT 20:18–20:19 parallels MRK 10:33–10:34, LUK 18:31–18:33

Other allusions to the cross

In the teaching of Jesus Christ

MAT 26:31 See also MAT 20:22 parallel MRK 10:38; MAT 26:2; MRK 2:19–2:20, MRK 10:45; LUK 9:44, LUK 22:42JHN 3:14, JHN 8:28, JHN 12:32–12:33 JHN 18:11

In history

MAT 17:9–17:13 John the Baptist's fate indicates what is to happen to Jesus Christ.

In the experience of Jesus Christ

MAT 26:12 parallel MRK 14:8 Jesus Christ's reaction to the woman anointing him seems to suggest that he will die the death of a criminal.

In the Lord's Supper

MAT 26:26–26:28 parallels MRK 14:22–14:25, LUK 22:19–22:20

God's perfect timing for Jesus Christ's death on the cross

JHN 2:4 See also JHN 7:6, JHN 7:8, JHN 7:30, JHN 8:20, JHN 13:1, JHN 17:1

The curse of the cross

DEU 21:22–21:23 To be nailed to a cross is the equivalent of being hanged on a tree. See also ACT 5:30; GAL 3:10–3:13; 1PE 2:24

title: 2412: cross, Gospel accounts of
content:
Each of the four Gospels provides a detailed account of Jesus Christ's death on the cross.

The way to the cross

Simon is compelled to carry the cross

MRK 15:21 parallels MAT 27:32, LUK 23:26

Women follow the cross and Jesus Christ is offered relief from pain

LUK 23:27–23:31 See also MAT 27:34 parallel MRK 15:23

The crucifixion of Jesus Christ

Jesus Christ crucified between two criminals

MRK 15:22–15:27 parallels MAT 27:33–27:38, LUK 23:32–23:43 “Golgotha” is a transliteration of the Aramaic word for “a skull”. Its actual location is uncertain.

The humiliation of Jesus Christ on the cross

MRK 15:29–15:32 parallels MAT 27:39–27:44, LUK 23:35–23:39

The words of Jesus Christ on the cross

JHN 19:30 See also MAT 27:46 parallel MRK 15:34; MAT 27:50; MRK 15:37; LUK 23:28–23:31, LUK 23:34, LUK 23:43, LUK 23:46; JHN 19:26–19:28

The death of Jesus Christ

MRK 15:33–15:37 parallels MAT 27:45–27:50, LUK 23:44–23:46 See also JHN 19:28–19:35 According to John, Jesus Christ was on the cross while the Passover lambs were being sacrificed.

Supernatural events at the cross

MRK 15:38 parallels MAT 27:51–27:53, LUK 23:45

Witnesses of the crucifixion

MRK 15:39–15:41 parallels MAT 27:54–27:56, LUK 23:47–23:49

title: 2413: cross, of Jesus Christ
content: See 2525: Jesus Christ, cross of

title: 2414: cross, centrality of
content:
The death of Jesus Christ on the cross is central to the Christian faith. Through the cross and resurrection of Christ, God achieved the redemption of believers and brought hope to the world.

The gospel as the ``message of the cross''

1CO 2:2 See also 1CO 1:17–1:18, 1CO 1:23; GAL 3:1

The cross redeems from the curse of the law

The cross brings reconciliation and justification

ROM 4:25, ROM 5:10 See also ROM 5:8–5:9; 1CO 15:3–15:4; EPH 2:16; COL 1:20–1:22

The cross destroys the power of Satan

COL 2:13–2:15 See also JHN 12:31, JHN 14:30; GAL 1:4; HEB 2:14–2:15; 1PE 3:21–3:22

The cross as a stumbling-block or offence

1CO 1:22–1:24 See also GAL 5:11, GAL 6:12

The cross unites believers with Jesus Christ

ROM 6:4–6:7 See also ROM 6:2, ROM 8:36; 1CO 15:30–15:31; 2CO 4:10–4:12, 2CO 5:14–5:15; GAL 2:20; EPH 2:14–2:16; COL 2:20, COL 3:1–3:3; 2TI 2:11

MAT 16:24 parallels MRK 8:34, LUK 9:23–9:24PHP 2:5–2:8 See also MAT 10:38; LUK 14:27; JHN 12:23–12:25; ROM 8:13; GAL 5:24, GAL 6:14; EPH 5:25–5:26; COL 3:5; 1PE 2:21, 1PE 2:24

title: 2420: gospel
content: The good news of God's redemption of sinful humanity through the life, death and resurrection of his Son Jesus Christ.

title: 2421: gospel, historical foundation of
content:
The gospel rests upon the history of Jesus Christ: his birth, obedient life, atoning death, physical resurrection from the dead and his ascension into heaven.

The incarnation of Jesus Christ

The historical facts

LUK 2:4–2:7 See also LUK 2:10–2:12; MAT 1:20–1:21; LUK 1:30–1:35

Its theological significance

JHN 1:14 See also JHN 1:9; ROM 8:3; 2CO 8:9; GAL 4:4–4:5; PHP 2:6–2:7; 1TI 3:16; HEB 2:14; 1JN 1:1–1:2

The sinless life of Jesus Christ

1PE 2:22 See also ISA 53:9; 2CO 5:21; HEB 4:15, HEB 7:26–7:28; 1JN 3:5

The death of Jesus Christ

An historical event

PHP 2:8 See also MAT 27:50 parallels MRK 15:37, LUK 23:46, JHN 19:30

Its redemptive significance

ROM 5:8–5:11 See also LUK 12:49–12:50, LUK 24:26; JHN 17:4; ACT 2:23; ROM 5:19; 1CO 1:23–1:24; 1PE 2:24

The resurrection of Jesus Christ

Its historical reality

1CO 15:3–15:8 See also MAT 28:1–28:8 parallels MRK 16:1–16:8, LUK 24:1–24:10, JHN 20:1–20:9; 2TI 2:8–2:10

Its implication for believers

1CO 15:14 See also ROM 4:25 in relation to justification; ROM 6:1–6:14 in relation to sanctification; 1CO 15:21, 1CO 15:42–15:49 in relation to the resurrection of believers

The ascension of Jesus Christ

ACT 1:9 See also MRK 16:19; LUK 24:50–24:51; ACT 1:1–1:2, ACT 2:33; ROM 8:34; COL 3:1; 1TI 3:16; HEB 1:3, HEB 12:2

title: 2422: gospel, confirmation of
content:
The trustworthiness of the gospel of Jesus Christ rests upon the fulfilment of OT prophecies concerning Jesus Christ, the apostolic witness to Jesus Christ and the divine witness to Jesus Christ during his ministry, culminating in his resurrection.

Prophecies of the OT concerning Jesus Christ

Fulfilment of prophecy recognised by Jesus Christ

MAT 26:54–26:56 See also MAT 26:24, MAT 26:31; LUK 4:21, LUK 21:22, LUK 24:25–24:27, LUK 24:44, LUK 24:46; JHN 13:18; PSA 41:9

Fulfilment of prophecy recognised by NT speakers and writers

ROM 3:21 See also LUK 1:68–1:70; ACT 3:18, ACT 10:43, ACT 26:22; ROM 1:2; 1CO 15:3–15:5; 1PE 1:10–1:12MAT 1:22–1:23; ISA 7:14; MAT 2:5–2:6; MIC 5:2; MAT 2:15; HOS 11:1; MAT 2:17–2:18; JER 31:15; MAT 2:23, MAT 4:14–4:16; ISA 9:1–9:2; MAT 8:17; ISA 53:4; MAT 12:17–12:21; ISA 42:1–42:4; MAT 21:4–21:5; ZEC 9:9; MAT 27:9–27:10; JHN 19:24; PSA 22:18; JHN 19:28, JHN 19:36; EXO 12:46; JHN 19:37; ZEC 12:10; ACT 2:25–2:28; PSA 16:8–16:11

Realisation of types

The sign of Jonah: MAT 12:38–12:41 parallel LUK 11:29–11:32; ; MAT 16:4; JHN 6:31–6:35, JHN 6:48–6:51; HEB 3:1–3:6 Jesus Christ as the greater Moses; ; HEB 6:20–7:10 Jesus Christ as the high priest after the order of Melchizedek

Fulfilment of ceremony and sacrifice

JHN 1:29 Jesus Christ fulfils all that the sacrificial system stood for. See also HEB 9:6–9:14; 1PE 1:18–1:19

Witness of the apostles to Jesus Christ

JHN 19:35 See also LUK 24:48; JHN 1:14, JHN 15:27, JHN 21:24; ACT 1:8, ACT 2:32, ACT 4:20, ACT 5:32, ACT 13:31; 1PE 5:1; 1JN 1:1–1:2, 1JN 4:14

Witness of God the Father to his Son

MAT 3:16–3:17 parallels MRK 1:9–1:11, LUK 3:21–3:22 See also JHN 1:32–1:34; MAT 17:5 parallels MRK 9:7, LUK 9:35; JHN 12:23–12:32; 2PE 1:17–1:18

Jesus Christ vindicated by his resurrection

ROM 1:1–1:4 See also ACT 2:24–2:28; PSA 16:8–16:11; ACT 13:30–13:35, ACT 17:31; ROM 8:11, ROM 10:9; 1CO 15:14–15:15; 1PE 1:21

title: 2423: gospel, essence of
content:
The chief characteristic and fundamental doctrine of the gospel is that Jesus Christ is both Lord and Saviour.

Jesus Christ as Lord

The universal lordship of Jesus Christ

MAT 28:18 See also DAN 7:13–7:14; LUK 10:22; JHN 3:35, JHN 17:2; ACT 10:36; ROM 14:9; 1CO 15:27; EPH 1:20–1:22; PHP 2:9–2:10; COL 1:15–1:20

Personal implications of the lordship of Jesus Christ

LUK 12:8–12:9; ROM 10:9 See also MAT 7:21–7:27 parallel LUK 6:46–6:49; JHN 13:13–13:14

The lordship of Jesus Christ can only be acknowledged through divine inspiration and revelation

1CO 12:3 See also JHN 16:13–16:15; 1JN 4:2–4:3

Jesus Christ as Saviour

LUK 2:11 See also JHN 1:29, JHN 4:42; ACT 5:31; 1TI 2:5–2:6; TIT 2:11–2:14; 1JN 4:14

Jesus Christ is the promised Messiah

MAT 1:20–1:23 See also ISA 7:14; PSA 130:8; ISA 53:11; ACT 13:23

Jesus Christ did everything that was required to save his people

JHN 19:30 He fulfilled all God's righteous requirements, made perfect atonement for sin and gained victory over death for all who believe in him. See also MAT 3:15; JHN 4:34, JHN 17:4; 1CO 15:20–15:22

Salvation comes only through Jesus Christ

JHN 14:6 See also ACT 4:12, ACT 10:43, ACT 16:30–16:31; 1TI 2:5

title: 2424: gospel, promises of
content:
To all who believe and submit to its demands, the promises of the gospel include forgiveness of sins, new life in Jesus Christ and adoption into the family of God.

Forgiveness of sins

The sin of God's people is imputed to God's Son

JHN 1:29 See also ISA 53:4–53:6; LUK 24:46–24:47; ACT 5:30–5:32, ACT 13:38; TIT 2:13–2:14; HEB 9:28; 1PE 2:24

The righteousness of God's Son is imputed to God's people

ROM 1:16–1:17 See also ROM 3:21–3:26, ROM 9:30; PHP 3:7–3:9

Peace with God

ROM 5:1–5:2 See also JHN 14:27; ROM 8:1–8:4, ROM 8:31–8:35

New birth

1PE 1:23–1:25 See also JHN 1:12–1:13, JHN 3:5–3:8; JAS 1:18

Eternal life

ROM 8:12–8:17 See also JHN 1:12–1:13; GAL 3:26, GAL 4:4–4:6; EPH 1:5

title: 2425: gospel, requirements of
content:
The gospel demands an obedient response to all that God has done for humanity in Jesus Christ. This includes faith in God, trust in the work of Jesus Christ, the repenting of sin, being baptised, and becoming like Christ through discipleship.

The requirement of faith

Belief in God

HEB 11:6 See also JHN 10:38, JHN 11:25–11:27, JHN 14:8–14:11

Trust in Jesus Christ

JHN 3:14–3:16 See also JHN 1:12–1:13, JHN 3:36, JHN 7:37–7:39, JHN 20:31; ACT 13:38–13:39, ACT 16:31; ROM 3:22

The requirement of repentance

A conscious change of mind and heart

ACT 3:17–3:20 See also PSA 51:17; JER 3:12–3:13, JER 6:16; LUK 18:13–18:14; ACT 17:30

Turning away from sin

ACT 8:22 See also 2CH 7:14; PSA 34:14; ISA 59:20; JER 25:4–25:6

Turning towards God

ACT 20:21 See also DEU 4:29–4:31, DEU 30:8–30:10; ISA 44:21–44:22, ISA 55:6–55:7; HOS 14:1–14:2; JAS 4:8–4:10

The requirement of baptism

ACT 2:38 See also MAT 28:18–28:20; ACT 8:12, ACT 8:36–8:38, ACT 10:47–10:48, ACT 19:1–19:5, ACT 22:16

The requirement of public confession of Jesus Christ

ROM 10:9–10:10 See also MAT 10:32 parallel LUK 12:8–12:9

The requirement of discipleship

Willingness to learn from Jesus Christ

MAT 11:28–11:30 See also JHN 13:14–13:15; PHP 2:5; 1PE 2:21

Willingness to obey Jesus Christ

JHN 14:15 See also JHN 14:21, JHN 14:23, JHN 15:10; 1JN 2:3–2:6, 1JN 3:21–3:24, 1JN 5:3; 2JN 1:6

Willingness to suffer for the sake of Jesus Christ

MAT 16:24 parallels MRK 8:34, LUK 9:23 See also ACT 14:21–14:22; PHP 1:29; 2TI 3:10–3:12; JAS 1:2; 1PE 3:14, 1PE 4:12–4:19

title: 2426: gospel, responses to
content:
The gospel cannot be subscribed to half-heartedly or in part; it must be either accepted or rejected.

The gospel calls for a positive response

ROM 10:5–10:13 See also ACT 13:38–13:39, ACT 16:29–16:32; ROM 3:21–3:26; JHN 6:41–6:52; 1JN 5:1, 1JN 5:5, 1JN 5:10–5:12

The command to accept the gospel

MAT 11:28; ACT 17:30 See also MAT 6:19–6:24 parallel LUK 11:34–11:36; MAT 7:7–7:12 parallel LUK 11:9–11:13; MAT 7:24–7:27 parallel LUK 6:47–6:49; JHN 4:13–4:14, JHN 6:35–6:40, JHN 8:31–8:32, JHN 8:36, JHN 10:9–10:10, JHN 11:25–11:26, JHN 12:44–12:46

Examples of people who accepted the gospel

MRK 1:16–1:18 parallel MAT 4:18–4:22 See also MRK 1:19–1:20; MAT 9:9 parallels MRK 2:14, LUK 5:27–5:28 Matthew was also known as Levi; ACT 2:41, ACT 11:19–11:21, ACT 13:46–13:48, ACT 16:13–16:15

Rejection of the gospel

Through lack of perception

JHN 12:37–12:41 See also MAT 13:13–13:15 parallels MRK 4:10–4:12, LUK 8:9–8:10; ISA 6:9; ROM 11:8; EPH 4:17–4:19

Through worldly distractions

MAT 13:22 parallels MRK 4:18–4:19, LUK 8:14 See also MAT 6:24 parallel LUK 16:13; 2TI 4:10; JAS 4:4; 1JN 2:15

Through indifference

MAT 12:30 parallel LUK 11:23

Through hostility to God

ROM 8:6–8:8 See also JHN 7:13–7:20, JHN 10:24–10:33; ACT 4:15–4:18, ACT 5:17–5:28, ACT 7:51–7:53, ACT 19:8–19:9, ACT 21:27–21:32; 1TH 2:14–2:16

Warnings against rejecting the gospel

HEB 2:1–2:4 See also MRK 8:36–8:38; LUK 12:5, LUK 12:16–12:21, LUK 13:22–13:30; JHN 12:47–12:50; HEB 6:4–6:6, HEB 10:29–10:31, HEB 12:25; 2PE 2:17–2:22

Examples of people who rejected the gospel

MAT 19:16–19:22 parallels MRK 10:17–10:22, LUK 18:18–18:23 See also ACT 5:29–5:33, ACT 7:51–7:58, ACT 17:32, ACT 13:44–13:47, ACT 19:23–19:28, ACT 24:24–24:25, ACT 26:24–26:28

The gospel evokes opposition

JHN 17:14; ACT 17:13 See also ACT 13:8, ACT 17:5–17:9; 1TH 2:14–2:16

The gospel evokes persecution

ACT 8:1–8:3; 1TH 3:7 See also ACT 13:49–13:50; 2TH 1:4

title: 2427: gospel, transmission of
content:
The gospel has been passed down through the NT witness to Jesus Christ, through the proclamation of the good news about Jesus Christ, and through the providential guidance of the Holy Spirit.

The NT witness to Jesus Christ

The events of Jesus Christ's life were carefully recorded

LUK 1:1–1:4 See also JHN 21:24; ACT 1:1–1:2

The apostles bore witness to the gospel

1CO 15:3–15:8; 2PE 1:16; 1JN 1:1–1:4 See also LUK 24:45–24:48; JHN 15:27; ACT 1:8, ACT 1:21–1:22, ACT 2:32, ACT 4:1–4:20, ACT 5:32, ACT 13:30–13:31; HEB 2:3–2:4; 1PE 5:1; 1JN 4:14

The proclamation of the gospel

ACT 5:42; ROM 10:14–10:15; 1CO 15:1–15:2; EPH 3:8–3:9 See also ACT 8:12; 1CO 1:21; GAL 1:23; 2TI 4:2; HEB 13:7

The guidance of the Holy Spirit in the transmission of the gospel

JHN 14:26, JHN 15:26 See also JHN 16:12–16:15; ACT 5:32; HEB 2:3–2:4; 1JN 5:6

title: 2428: gospel, descriptions of
content:
The beauty, authority and importance of the gospel can be seen from the way it is described in Scripture.

In the Gospels

As good and joyful news

MAT 4:23 See also MRK 1:14, MRK 16:15; LUK 2:10–2:11

As being of heavenly origin

JHN 17:16–17:18 See also JHN 8:28, JHN 12:49–12:50, JHN 14:10, JHN 14:24

As words of life

JHN 6:63 See also JHN 3:11–3:15

As being complete and gloriously rich

COL 1:25–1:27 See also ROM 10:12–10:13; EPH 3:8

In the OT

ISA 52:7–52:10 See also ISA 40:9, ISA 41:27, ISA 61:1; NAM 1:15

In the NT

1TI 1:11 See also ACT 20:24; ROM 1:16–1:17, ROM 10:15; ISA 52:7; ROM 15:18–15:19; EPH 1:13; REV 14:6
