title: 2505: Jesus Christ, ascension of
content:
The return of Jesus Christ to his Father in order to establish his kingdom, having completed his work on earth.

Jesus Christ's ascension described

ACT 1:9–1:11 As in the OT, the cloud symbolises God's presence. See also MRK 16:19; LUK 24:51; ACT 1:2; EPH 4:10; 1TI 3:16; HEB 9:24; 1PE 3:22; REV 12:5

Predictions of Jesus Christ's ascension

In the OT

EPH 4:7–4:8 See also PSA 68:18

Jesus Christ looks forward to his ascension as his return to his Father

JHN 16:28 See also JHN 6:62, JHN 7:33, JHN 14:28, JHN 16:5, JHN 16:10, JHN 17:11, JHN 17:13, JHN 20:17

Jesus Christ establishes his kingdom by his ascension

He ascended to send the Holy Spirit

JHN 16:7 See also JHN 7:39, JHN 14:16–14:19, JHN 14:25–14:26, JHN 15:26–15:27; ACT 2:33

He ascended to prepare a place for his people

JHN 14:2–14:4

He ascended to exaltation and glory

See also LUK 24:26; ACT 2:33; EPH 1:20–1:21; 1PE 3:22

He ascended to exercise his high priestly ministry

HEB 4:14 See also HEB 7:25, HEB 9:24

He ascended so that believers may follow him

1TH 4:17

Jesus Christ's ascension indicates the manner of his return

ACT 1:11 See also MAT 24:30 parallels MRK 13:26, LUK 21:27; REV 1:7

title: 2510: Jesus Christ, baptism of
content:
In obedience to God's will Jesus Christ was baptised in the River Jordan by John. The event was sealed by the descent of the Holy Spirit and the Father's voice of approval.

John tries to prevent Jesus Christ from being baptised

MAT 3:13–3:14 John was baptising repentant sinners but he knew that Jesus Christ was sinless.

The necessity of Jesus Christ's baptism

MAT 3:15 See also MRK 10:38; LUK 12:50

The events surrounding Jesus Christ's baptism

The baptism itself

MRK 1:9 parallels MAT 3:13, LUK 3:21

Jesus Christ prays while being baptised

LUK 3:21

The Holy Spirit descends on Jesus Christ

MRK 1:10 parallels MAT 3:16, LUK 3:22 The Holy Spirit came upon Jesus Christ to equip him for his ministry which was about to begin and to give him strength at a time of temptation. See also JHN 1:32–1:34

The voice of the Father is heard

MAT 3:17 parallels MRK 1:11, LUK 3:22 These words, which are an allusion to Ps 2:7 and Isa 42:1, were repeated at the transfiguration. See also MAT 17:5 parallels MRK 9:7, LUK 9:35

resource.label: 3269: Holy Spirit, in life of Jesus Christ

title: 2515: Jesus Christ, birth of
content:
Jesus Christ was born to a poor unmarried Jewish couple in the village of Bethlehem. As Son of God, he was conceived by the Holy Spirit in the womb of his mother Mary.

Jesus Christ's birth is prophesied in the OT

The prophecy of a coming ruler

ISA 9:6–9:7

The prophecy of a virgin's son

ISA 7:14

The prophecy concerning his birthplace

MIC 5:2 “Bethlehem Ephrathah” is the village where David grew up (“the town of David”).

Jesus Christ was conceived by the Holy Spirit and born of a virgin

MAT 1:18, MAT 1:23 See also MAT 1:20; LUK 1:34–1:35

Jesus Christ's incarnation

JHN 1:14; GAL 4:4 See also ROM 8:3; 2CO 8:9; PHP 2:7–2:8; 1TI 3:16; 1JN 4:2

The circumstances of Jesus Christ's birth

An angel informs Mary that she is to be the mother of the Messiah

LUK 1:26–1:38

Mary responds in praise

See also LUK 1:46–1:55

An angel explains to Joseph why Mary's baby is so special

MAT 1:18–1:25

The birth of Jesus Christ takes place

LUK 2:1–2:7

Angels announce Jesus Christ's birth to some shepherds

See also LUK 2:8–2:20

The Magi search for Jesus Christ's birthplace and Herod tries to kill him

MAT 2:1–2:18 These events could have taken place at any time in Jesus Christ's first two years.

title: 2520: Jesus Christ, childhood of
content:
As an infant Jesus Christ was consecrated in the temple, accompanied by prophetic utterances regarding his future ministry. His childhood was characterised by a growth in wisdom and grace and, in particular, a strong desire to learn of God his Father and to understand the Scriptures.

Jesus Christ was consecrated as an infant

LUK 2:22 See also LUK 2:23–2:24

Indications of Jesus Christ's future ministry were revealed

Through the words of Simeon

LUK 2:30–2:32 See also LUK 2:25–2:29, LUK 2:34–2:35

Through the words of Anna

LUK 2:38

The visit of the Magi

See also MAT 2:1–2:12

The escape to Egypt

MAT 2:13–2:15

The return from Egypt

MAT 2:19–2:20 See also MAT 2:15; HOS 11:1; MAT 2:21

Jesus Christ lives in Nazareth

MAT 2:22–2:23 See also LUK 2:39

Jesus Christ grows up

LUK 2:40 See also LUK 2:52

Jesus Christ visited the temple

He celebrates the Passover there

LUK 2:41–2:42

He stays behind in the temple

LUK 2:43–2:45, LUK 2:48–2:50

He displays his understanding of the Scriptures there

LUK 2:46–2:47

The obedience of Jesus Christ

LUK 2:51

Mary and Joseph remember the sayings and events of Jesus Christ's childhood

See also LUK 2:19, LUK 2:33, LUK 2:51

title: 2525: Jesus Christ, cross of
content:
The death of Jesus Christ is of central importance to the Christian faith. Through Christ's death, sinners are reconciled to God and to their fellow human beings. Paul summarises the gospel as “the message of the cross”.

Jesus Christ foretold his death on the cross

MAT 20:18–20:19 parallels MRK 10:33–10:34, LUK 18:31–18:32 See also MAT 26:2; JHN 3:14–3:15, JHN 8:28, JHN 12:32–12:33, JHN 18:32

The facts of Jesus Christ's death on the cross

Carrying Jesus Christ's cross

JHN 19:17 Though Jesus Christ began to carry his cross, the Romans soon forced Simon of Cyrene to continue with it. See also MAT 27:32 parallels MRK 15:21, LUK 23:26

Jesus Christ refused the comfort of wine, which would have lessened the pains of his suffering

MAT 27:34 parallel MRK 15:23

Jesus Christ was nailed to the cross

LUK 23:33 parallels MAT 27:35, MRK 15:24–15:25, JHN 19:18

Jesus Christ's words from the cross

See also LUK 23:34, LUK 23:43; JHN 19:26–19:27; MAT 27:46 parallel MRK 15:34; PSA 22:1; LUK 23:46; JHN 19:28–19:30

Signs accompanying Jesus Christ's death on the cross

Darkness over the whole land for three hours: MAT 27:45; ; MRK 15:33 parallel LUK 23:44–23:45; MAT 27:51 parallels MRK 15:38, LUK 23:45; ; MAT 27:52–27:53 the dead come back to life

People at the scene of the cross

The Roman centurion and his men: MAT 27:35–27:36, MAT 27:54 parallels MRK 15:39, LUK 23:47; MAT 27:38 parallels MRK 15:27, LUK 23:32–23:33, JHN 19:18; ; MAT 27:44; ; LUK 23:39–23:43; MAT 27:39–27:43 parallels MRK 15:29–15:32, LUK 23:35–23:37 people who insulted and mocked Jesus Christ; MAT 27:55–27:56 parallels MRK 15:40–15:41, LUK 23:27–23:31, JHN 19:25; ; LUK 23:49; JHN 19:26–19:27 John

Responsibility for the cross

The cross as God's plan

ACT 2:23 See also ISA 53:10; MAT 26:39 parallels MRK 14:36, LUK 22:42; MAT 26:42; JHN 14:30–14:31; ACT 4:25–4:28; PSA 2:1–2:2; REV 13:8

The crowd's call for Jesus Christ's crucifixion

MAT 27:21–27:22 parallels MRK 15:12–15:14, LUK 23:18–23:21, JHN 19:15; ; MAT 27:25; ; ACT 2:36; ; 1TH 2:14–2:15

Jewish and Roman leaders' responsibility

MAT 27:1, MAT 27:20 parallel MRK 15:11; ; MAT 27:26 parallels MRK 15:15, JHN 19:16; ; LUK 23:24–23:25; ; JHN 19:6–19:7; ; ACT 4:10; ; 1CO 2:8

The triumph of the cross

COL 2:15

The cross brings glory to Jesus Christ

JHN 17:1 Jesus Christ refers to his approaching death, the climax of his earthly life. See also JHN 12:28, JHN 13:31–13:32, JHN 17:5; PHP 2:8–2:11; HEB 12:2; REV 5:8–5:14

The cross brings reconciliation

EPH 2:13–2:17 See also ROM 5:9–5:10; 2CO 5:18–5:19; COL 1:20

Identification with the crucified Christ releases believers from their old way of life

GAL 2:20 See also ROM 6:5–6:13; GAL 5:24–5:25, GAL 6:14; COL 2:13–2:14

Jesus Christ's cross is central to the Christian message

1CO 2:2 See also ACT 2:23–2:24, ACT 3:13, ACT 5:30, ACT 13:27–13:29; GAL 3:1

Jesus Christ's cross is the symbol of Christian discipleship

MAT 16:24 parallels MRK 8:34, LUK 9:23 See also MAT 10:38; JHN 21:18–21:19

title: 2530: Jesus Christ, death of
content:
The death of Jesus Christ by crucifixion is of central importance to the NT. Through the faithful, obedient death of Christ, God grants sinners forgiveness and eternal life. The Christian sacraments of baptism and the Lord's Supper focus upon the death of Christ.

Jesus Christ's death was foretold

In time and in eternity

REV 13:8 See also ISA 53:10–53:12; ZEC 12:10; JHN 1:29, JHN 1:36

By Jesus Christ himself

MAT 16:21 parallels MRK 8:31, LUK 9:22 See also MAT 17:22–17:23, MAT 26:12 parallel JHN 12:7; JHN 16:16, JHN 16:28, JHN 18:4

Jesus Christ willingly planned to give his life

JHN 10:11 See also JHN 10:15, JHN 10:17–10:18, JHN 13:1, JHN 15:13; 1JN 3:16

Plots to kill Jesus Christ

MRK 3:6 parallel MAT 12:14 See also MAT 26:4 parallel MRK 14:1; LUK 13:31, LUK 19:47; JHN 5:18, JHN 7:25, JHN 8:37, JHN 8:40

The manner of Jesus Christ's death

Jesus Christ died by crucifixion

LUK 23:33 parallels MAT 27:35, MRK 15:24–15:25

The moment of death

MRK 15:37 parallels MAT 27:50, LUK 23:46, JHN 19:30 See also JHN 19:33

Events accompanying Jesus Christ's death

MAT 27:45 Darkness descended on the land; ; MAT 27:51 The curtain of the temple was split; ; MAT 27:52–27:53 The dead emerged from their tombs.

Jesus Christ's burial

MAT 27:58–27:60 parallels MRK 15:43–15:46, LUK 23:51–23:53, JHN 19:38–19:42

The unique character of Jesus Christ's death

Jesus Christ's body did not decay

ACT 2:31 See also PSA 16:10; ACT 2:27, ACT 13:36–13:37

Jesus Christ descended to the dead

ROM 1:4 See also 1PE 3:18–3:22

Jesus Christ was raised bodily from the dead

LUK 24:36–24:40 See also JHN 21:12–21:13; ACT 10:41, ACT 25:19

Death had no power over Jesus Christ

ACT 2:24 See also ROM 6:9–6:10; REV 1:18

Jesus Christ's death was a unique sacrifice

HEB 7:27 See also 1CO 5:7; EPH 5:2; HEB 9:26, HEB 9:28, HEB 10:10, HEB 10:12; 1JN 2:2, 1JN 4:10

Achievements of Jesus Christ's death

Jesus Christ's death establishes the new covenant

LUK 22:20 parallels MAT 26:28, MRK 14:24, 1CO 11:25 See also HEB 9:15, HEB 13:20

Jesus Christ's death is a victory

COL 2:14–2:15 See also HEB 2:9, HEB 2:14–2:15; REV 12:11

Jesus Christ's death brings redemption

REV 5:9 See also MRK 10:45 parallel MAT 20:28; ACT 20:28; GAL 3:13; EPH 1:7; TIT 2:14; 1PE 1:18–1:19

Jesus Christ's death brings forgiveness and cleansing

1JN 1:7 See also HEB 9:14; REV 1:5, REV 7:14

Jesus Christ's death brings sanctification

HEB 13:12 See also HEB 10:14; 1PE 1:2

Believers identify with Jesus Christ in his death

ROM 6:3–6:8 See also 2CO 5:14–5:15; GAL 2:20; COL 2:11; 2TI 2:11

title: 2535: Jesus Christ, family of
content:
Jesus Christ belonged to a human family and experienced family relationships during his earthly ministry. Such relationships underline the fact of Christ's humanity.

Jesus Christ was born into a family

He is placed in a family tree

MAT 1:1–1:17; ; LUK 1:34–1:37, LUK 3:23–3:38

His birth was a natural one

LUK 2:5–2:7 Jesus Christ's conception was miraculous, his birth natural. See also LUK 2:21–2:22 see Lev 12:2–4 for the instructions concerning purification after childbirth

He grew up within a family

LUK 2:48 See also LUK 2:51–2:52

Jesus Christ's family is mentioned during his earthly ministry

MAT 13:55–13:56 parallel MRK 6:3 See also MAT 12:46–12:47 parallels MRK 3:31–3:32, LUK 8:19–8:20; JHN 2:1, JHN 2:12

Jesus Christ's natural brothers

James

GAL 1:19 This verse gives support to the traditional view that James, elder at Jerusalem and writer of the NT letter which bears his name was the Lord's brother. (Other references to him include Ac 15:13; 21:18; 1Co 15:7; Jas 1:1.). See also MAT 13:55 parallel MRK 6:3

Jude

JUD 1:1 See also MAT 13:55 parallel MRK 6:3 Jude is understood to be a variation of Judas.

The nature of Jesus Christ's relationships with his family

He was obedient to and caring towards his parents

LUK 2:51 See also JHN 19:26–19:27

The will of his heavenly Father took precedence over the wishes of his natural family

JHN 2:3–2:4 In John's Gospel, Jesus Christ's “time” (or “hour”) is his cross. Here Christ insists that only his heavenly Father can dictate the course of his ministry. See also MAT 12:48–12:50 parallel MRK 3:33–3:35; LUK 2:48–2:49; JHN 7:6–7:8

His family did not fully understand his ministry

JHN 7:3–7:5 See also MRK 3:21

Jesus Christ's natural family were among the first Christians

ACT 1:14 See also 1CO 9:5

title: 2540: Jesus Christ, genealogy of
content:
Jesus Christ's human ancestry is traced back in two separate lines to David and thence back to Adam. Jesus' descent from David is of importance for his Messianic claims.

Jesus Christ is descended from Adam

LUK 3:23–3:38

Jesus Christ is descended from Abraham

GAL 3:16 See also ACT 3:25–3:26

Jesus Christ is descended from Isaac

GEN 21:12, GEN 26:2–26:4; ; GAL 3:16

Jesus Christ is descended from Jacob

GEN 28:13–28:14; ; MAT 1:2; ; LUK 3:34

Jesus Christ is descended from the tribe of Judah

HEB 7:14 See also RUT 4:18–4:22

Jesus Christ is descended from Jesse

ISA 11:1; ROM 15:12

Jesus Christ is descended from David

Jesus Christ's human lineage

MAT 1:1–1:17; LUK 2:4 See also 1CH 3:10–3:19; LUK 3:23–3:31

Jesus Christ as the Son of David

REV 22:16 See also MAT 1:20, MAT 20:30–20:31 parallels MRK 10:47–10:48, LUK 18:38–18:39; MAT 21:9, MAT 21:15; LUK 1:27, LUK 1:32; JHN 7:42

Jesus Christ is greater than David

MAT 22:41–22:45 parallels MRK 12:35–12:37, LUK 20:41–20:44 See also PSA 110:1; REV 5:5, REV 22:16

Jesus Christ is greater than Solomon

MAT 12:42 parallel LUK 11:31

Jesus Christ as the son of Joseph and Mary

MAT 13:55 parallel MRK 6:3JHN 6:42 See also MAT 1:16; LUK 4:22; JHN 1:45

title: 2545: Jesus Christ, opposition to
content:
Jesus Christ encountered criticism, grumbling and plotting because he was seen as a threat to the religious and political hierarchy, and because he exposed sin.

Opposition by different groups

By family and friends

LUK 4:24–4:29 See also MAT 13:55–13:57 parallel MRK 6:3–6:4; MRK 3:21; JHN 7:3–7:5

By religious leaders

MAT 12:14 parallels MRK 3:6, LUK 6:11 See also JHN 11:47–11:53

By political leaders

MAT 2:13–2:14; ; LUK 13:31

Opposition through criticism and accusation

Criticism of Jesus Christ's choice of company

MAT 9:11 parallels MRK 2:16, LUK 5:30 See also LUK 7:39, LUK 19:7

Criticism for breaking with tradition

MAT 15:1–15:2 parallel MRK 7:5 Though criticism is directed at the disciples, it is really against Jesus Christ; ; LUK 10:16, LUK 11:38

Accusations of Sabbath-breaking

JHN 5:16 See also MAT 12:2 parallels MRK 2:24, LUK 6:2; MAT 12:10 parallels MRK 3:2, LUK 6:7; LUK 13:14, LUK 14:1

Accusations of blasphemy

JHN 10:33 See also MAT 9:3 parallels MRK 2:6–2:7, LUK 5:21; MAT 26:65 parallel MRK 14:64; JHN 5:18, JHN 10:36

Accusations of being demon-possessed

JHN 10:20 See also MAT 12:24 parallels MRK 3:22, LUK 11:15; JHN 7:20, JHN 8:48

Reasons for opposing Jesus Christ

Because of hatred

JHN 15:23–15:25 See also JHN 7:7, JHN 8:40, JHN 15:18

Because people's minds are closed

JHN 8:43–8:44 See also JHN 8:37; 2CO 4:4

Jesus Christ knew where opposition would lead

MAT 16:21 parallels MRK 8:31–8:32, LUK 9:22 See also MAT 17:22–17:23 parallel MRK 9:31; MAT 20:17–20:19 parallels MRK 10:32–10:34, LUK 18:31–18:32; LUK 9:44

Unsuccessful opposition before the crucifixion

Early attempts to arrest Jesus Christ

JHN 10:39 See also JHN 7:30, JHN 7:32, JHN 7:44

Attempts on Jesus Christ's life

JHN 8:59 See also LUK 4:28–4:30; JHN 7:19, JHN 10:31

Opposition that led to the crucifixion

Plots by the Jewish leaders

MAT 26:3–26:4 parallels MRK 14:1–14:2, LUK 22:2 See also MAT 21:46 parallels MRK 12:12, LUK 20:19; JHN 11:57

The chief priests stirred up the crowd

MAT 27:20–27:23 parallels MRK 15:11–15:14, LUK 23:21 See also JHN 19:15

Rejection by political authorities

MAT 27:26 parallels MRK 15:15, LUK 23:24–23:25 See also LUK 23:7–23:12; JHN 19:16; ACT 4:25–4:27; PSA 2:1–2:2

The mockery of the Roman soldiers

MAT 27:27–27:31 parallel MRK 15:16–15:20

The betrayal by Judas Iscariot

MAT 26:14–26:16 parallels MRK 14:10–14:11, LUK 22:3–22:6; ; MAT 26:47–26:50 parallels MRK 14:43–14:45, LUK 22:47–22:48; ; JHN 18:2–18:5

Opposition at the crucifixion

MAT 27:39–27:44 parallels MRK 15:29–15:32, LUK 23:35–23:39; ; JHN 19:21

Opposition to Jesus Christ in apostolic preaching

ACT 3:13–3:15 See also ACT 2:23, ACT 4:10, ACT 5:30, ACT 7:52–7:53, ACT 10:39, ACT 13:27–13:29

Saul of Tarsus opposes Jesus Christ

ACT 9:4–9:5 See also ACT 22:7–22:8, ACT 26:14–26:15

title: 2550: Jesus Christ, pre-existence of
content: See 2018: Jesus Christ, divinity of

title: 2555: Jesus Christ, resurrection appearances of
content:
Jesus Christ appeared to various groups and individuals on several occasions after his death, prior to his ascension into heaven.

The resurrection appearances of Jesus Christ on the third day

To Mary Magdalene

JHN 20:10–20:18 See also MRK 16:9–16:11

To the women at the tomb

MAT 28:1–28:10 parallels MRK 16:1–16:8, LUK 24:1–24:12

To Peter

LUK 24:34; ; 1CO 15:5

To the two travellers to Emmaus

MRK 16:12–16:13; ; LUK 24:13–24:16, LUK 24:30–24:32

To the disciples in the upper room

MRK 16:14 parallels LUK 24:36, JHN 20:19

Further appearances of Jesus Christ

To the disciples in the upper room

See also JHN 20:26; 1CO 15:5

Other appearances

JHN 21:1 See also MAT 28:16–28:17; 1CO 15:6–15:7

Jesus Christ appears at his ascension

MRK 16:19; ; LUK 24:50–24:51; ; ACT 1:9

Jesus Christ's resurrection body

It was different from his pre-crucifixion body

JHN 20:26 This suggests that the resurrected body of Jesus Christ was not restricted by natural laws: closed doors provided no barrier to Jesus Christ's bodily appearance to his disciples. See also MRK 16:12 The failure to recognise Jesus Christ may be an indication of his changed appearance but also might emphasise the disciples' own sorrow and lack of faith; JHN 20:14, JHN 20:19 parallel LUK 24:36

It was a body of flesh and blood

LUK 24:39 See also LUK 24:42–24:43; JHN 20:20

Jesus Christ foretold his resurrection appearances

JHN 16:16

Responses to Jesus Christ's resurrection appearances

Fear and alarm

LUK 24:37 See also MAT 28:10

Doubt and disbelief

MRK 16:11 See also MAT 28:17; MRK 16:13–16:14; LUK 24:11–24:12; JHN 20:9, JHN 20:13–20:14, JHN 20:25

Belief and joy

MAT 28:8 See also LUK 24:31–24:32, LUK 24:41; JHN 20:16, JHN 20:18, JHN 20:27–20:29

Understanding and worship

MAT 28:9 See also LUK 24:8, LUK 24:45–24:47; JHN 2:22; ACT 2:31–2:33

The significance of Jesus Christ's resurrection appearances

They gave proof of Jesus Christ's deity

JHN 20:28–20:29 See also LUK 24:31–24:34; JHN 20:8; ACT 10:41–10:42

Christian life and faith depend on the trustworthiness of the witnesses' testimony

1CO 15:17 See also JHN 20:31; ACT 3:15; ROM 1:4; 1CO 15:1–15:58; 1TH 4:14; 1PE 1:3

title: 2560: Jesus Christ, resurrection of
content:
True Christian preaching is centred on the fact that God raised Jesus Christ from the dead so that believers may have victory over sin and death and receive the blessings of eternal life.

The resurrection of Jesus Christ is foretold

In Scripture

1CO 15:3–15:4 See also PSA 16:10, PSA 49:15; ISA 53:10–53:12; HOS 6:2; LUK 24:46; ACT 2:29–2:31, ACT 26:22–26:23

By Jesus Christ

MAT 16:21 parallels MRK 8:31, LUK 9:22 See also MAT 12:40 the sign of Jonah; MAT 17:9 parallel MRK 9:9 the transfiguration; MAT 17:22–17:23 parallel MRK 9:31; MAT 20:18–20:19 parallels MRK 10:33–10:34, LUK 18:31–18:33; MAT 26:32 parallel MRK 14:28; JHN 2:19–2:22 Jesus Christ clears the temple; JHN 16:16

The resurrection is preached by the apostles

ACT 4:33 See also ACT 2:24–2:32, ACT 10:40–10:41, ACT 17:2–17:3, ACT 17:18, ACT 17:31

The certainty of the resurrection

ACT 1:3 See also 1CO 15:3–15:8LUK 24:36–24:43; JHN 20:26–20:28

The necessity of the resurrection

1CO 15:17 See also JHN 20:9

The results of Jesus Christ's resurrection

God's power is demonstrated

EPH 1:19–1:20 See also ACT 2:24; 1CO 6:14

The sonship of Jesus Christ is declared

ROM 1:4 See also ACT 13:33

The lordship of Jesus Christ is declared

ROM 14:9 ACT 5:30–5:31; ; EPH 1:20–1:22; 2CO 5:15

The destruction of death's power is declared

REV 1:18 See also ACT 2:24; ROM 6:9

The benefits of Jesus Christ's resurrection for believers

It is the foundation of salvation

ROM 4:25 See also JHN 11:25–11:26; ROM 10:9; 1PE 1:21, 1PE 3:21

It provides the power to live for God

ROM 6:4 See also ROM 7:4, ROM 8:11; EPH 2:4–2:7; PHP 3:8–3:11; COL 2:12, COL 3:1; HEB 13:20–13:21

The intercession of Jesus Christ depends upon it

ROM 8:34 See also HEB 7:25

It brings assurance of resurrection to eternal life

1CO 6:14 See also ROM 6:5; 1CO 15:20–15:22; 2CO 4:14; 1TH 4:14; 1PE 1:3–1:4

resource.label: 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of

title: 2565: Jesus Christ, second coming of
content:
Jesus Christ will return visibly and in glory at the end of history to raise the dead, judge the world, destroy all evil and opposition to God and consummate his kingdom. Believers are encouraged to be prepared for his return, waiting with eagerness and joy.

The second coming is foretold

In the OT

DAN 7:13–7:14 See also PSA 72:2–72:4; ISA 2:2–2:4 parallel MIC 4:1–4:3; ISA 11:4–11:9, ISA 40:5

By Jesus Christ

MAT 24:30 parallels MRK 13:26, LUK 21:27 See also MAT 16:27, MAT 25:31; MRK 14:62; LUK 12:40; JHN 14:3, JHN 14:28

By the angels at Jesus Christ's ascension

ACT 1:11

By the apostles

PHP 3:20 See also ACT 3:20–3:21; 1CO 1:7, 1CO 11:26; 1TH 1:10, 1TH 3:13, 1TH 4:16; 2TH 1:7–1:10; HEB 9:28

NT terms for the second coming

``Parousia''

Meaning literally presence'' orcoming'', the term was used in the NT world of a royal visit: MAT 24:3; ; JAS 5:7–5:8; ; 2PE 3:4; ; 1JN 2:28

``Apocalypsis''

Meaning literally unveiling'',apocalypsis'' suggests that the second coming will make visible that which is already true of Jesus Christ but presently hidden from human sight: LUK 17:30; ; 1CO 1:7; ; 2TH 1:7; ; 1PE 1:7, 1PE 4:13

``Epiphaneia''

Meaning literally appearing'' ormanifestation'', the term suggests coming out into view from a hidden background: 2TH 2:8; ; 1TI 6:14; ; 2TI 4:1; ; TIT 2:13

Events that will precede the second coming

The universal preaching of the gospel

MAT 24:14 parallel MRK 13:10 See also MAT 28:19–28:20; ACT 1:7–1:8

The persecution of believers

MAT 24:9 parallels MRK 13:9, LUK 21:12 See also MAT 24:21 parallel MRK 13:19; MAT 24:22 parallel MRK 13:20; MRK 13:12–13:13 parallels MAT 24:12–24:13, LUK 21:16–21:17; 2TI 3:1; REV 7:14

Apostasy, nominal religion and godlessness

1TI 4:1 See also MAT 24:10; 2TI 3:1–3:5; 2PE 3:3–3:4

The revelation of the antichrist

1JN 2:18 The antichrist is a name given to the one who will lead opposition to God's kingdom. It appears to refer to an individual but may denote a corporate movement. See also DAN 7:20–7:22; 2TH 2:3–2:4; 1JN 4:3; REV 13:1–13:10

The appearance of false Christs and false prophets

MAT 24:24 parallel MRK 13:22 See also MAT 7:15, MAT 24:5 parallels MRK 13:6, LUK 21:8; MAT 24:11; LUK 17:23; 2JN 1:7

Wars and rumours of wars

MAT 24:6–24:7 parallels MRK 13:7–13:8, LUK 21:9–21:10

Famines and earthquakes

MAT 24:7–24:8 parallels MRK 13:8, LUK 21:11

Cosmic changes

MAT 24:29 parallels MRK 13:24, LUK 21:25–21:26

The timing of the second coming

At God's appointed time

ACT 3:20–3:21 See also MAT 24:36 parallel MRK 13:32; LUK 17:20–17:30; ACT 1:7; 2PE 3:9

Unexpectedly

1TH 5:1–5:2 See also MAT 24:27 parallel LUK 17:24; MAT 24:43–24:44 parallel LUK 12:39–12:40; 2PE 3:10; REV 3:3, REV 16:15

After a delay

MAT 25:5 Part of a parable concerning the coming of Jesus Christ who is represented by the bridegroom. See also MAT 25:19; 2PE 3:3–3:9

Imminently

MAT 24:30–24:34 parallels MRK 13:26–13:30, LUK 21:27–21:32 See also JHN 21:22; ROM 13:11–13:12; JAS 5:8–5:9; REV 3:11, REV 22:7, REV 22:20

The manner of the second coming

Jesus Christ's personal return

ACT 1:11 See also ACT 3:20; 1TH 4:16; HEB 9:28

A visible and glorious return

REV 1:7 See also MAT 24:30 parallels MRK 13:26, LUK 21:27; MAT 26:64; ACT 1:9

With the sound of a trumpet

MAT 24:31; ; 1CO 15:52; ; 1TH 4:16

With angels

MAT 16:27, MAT 24:31; ; 2TH 1:7

The purpose of the second coming

To raise the dead

1CO 15:22–15:23 See also JHN 5:28–5:29; 1CO 15:52; 1TH 4:16

To judge all people

PSA 96:13 See also PSA 98:9; MAT 16:27, MAT 25:31–25:32; 2TI 4:1; REV 20:11–20:15, REV 22:12

To judge the wicked

2TH 1:7–1:10 See also MAT 24:51; ROM 2:5; 2PE 3:7; JUD 1:15

To separate the wicked from the righteous

MAT 25:31–25:32 See also MAT 24:40–24:41 parallel LUK 17:34–17:35

To destroy all evil and opposition to God's kingdom

2TH 2:8 See also 1CO 15:24–15:26; REV 20:10, REV 20:14

To reward believers

MAT 24:46–24:47; ; LUK 12:37; ; 2TI 4:8; ; 1PE 5:4; ; 1JN 3:1

To gather the church together

JHN 14:3; ; 1TH 4:17; ; 2TH 2:1

To bring final salvation

LUK 21:28; ; HEB 9:28

To transform the saints into his likeness

1CO 15:51–15:52; ; PHP 3:20–3:21; ; COL 3:4

To end the present age

MAT 24:3; ; 1CO 15:24; ; 2PE 3:10, 2PE 3:12

To restore and renew all things

ACT 3:21; ; 2PE 3:13; ; REV 21:1–21:5

Reactions at Jesus Christ's second coming

MAT 24:30 all peoples will mourn; ; 2TH 1:10 His people will marvel.

Present implications of the second coming for believers

They must always be watchful and ready

MAT 24:42–24:44 See also MAT 25:10; MRK 13:35; LUK 12:35–12:36

They must be faithful stewards

MAT 25:14–25:30 parallel LUK 19:12–19:27

They must eagerly expect his coming

1CO 1:7 See also LUK 21:28; PHP 3:20; TIT 2:13

They must endure suffering with patient endurance

MAT 24:13; ; 1PE 1:6–1:7

They must wait patiently

1TH 1:10; ; JAS 5:7–5:8

It is an incentive to holiness

1JN 3:2–3:3 See also COL 3:4–3:5; 1TI 6:14; 2PE 3:11; 1JN 2:23, 1JN 2:28

It is an incentive to mission

2TI 4:1–4:2 See also 2PE 3:12

title: 2570: Jesus Christ, suffering of
content:
Jesus Christ's life was characterised by suffering, though the worst experiences were reserved for his final days. His sufferings are both redemptive and an example to believers.

Jesus Christ's sufferings foretold

In the OT

PSA 22:6–22:8, PSA 22:16–22:18; ; ISA 50:6, ISA 52:13–53:12; ; ZEC 9:9–9:10, ZEC 12:10, ZEC 13:7; ; 1PE 1:11

In Jesus Christ's predictions

MAT 16:21 parallels MRK 8:31, LUK 9:22 See also MAT 20:17–20:19 parallels MRK 10:32–10:34, LUK 18:31–18:32; JHN 12:32–12:33

Jesus Christ's suffering during his lifetime

As a child

MAT 2:13–2:15

Because of his family

MRK 3:20–3:21 See also JHN 7:3–7:5

Because of the crowds' unbelief

MRK 9:19 parallels MAT 17:17, LUK 9:41 See also MAT 12:39 parallel LUK 11:29; MRK 8:11–8:12

Because of the disciples' slowness

MRK 8:17–8:21 parallel MAT 16:8–16:11

Because of the religious leaders

MAT 12:14 parallels MRK 3:6, LUK 6:11 See also JHN 5:18, JHN 7:1, JHN 8:48

Because of human suffering

LUK 19:41 See also MRK 7:34; LUK 7:13; JHN 11:33–11:35

Jesus Christ's suffering at the time of his death

He was inwardly troubled

JHN 12:27 See also MAT 26:36–26:42 parallels MRK 14:32–14:39, LUK 22:40–22:44; JHN 13:21

He was betrayed

MAT 26:21–26:25 parallels MRK 14:17–14:21, LUK 22:21–22:23; ; MAT 26:47–26:49 parallels MRK 14:43–14:45, LUK 22:47–22:48, JHN 18:2–18:5; ; JHN 13:18–13:30

He was humiliated

title: 2580: Jesus Christ, transfiguration of
content:
The revelation of the glory of Jesus Christ, shortly before his death, at which his disciples caught sight of him in his full majesty. The transfiguration brought home to the disciples that Jesus Christ is the Son of God.

The glory of Jesus Christ is revealed at the transfiguration

Jesus Christ prepares for his glory to be revealed

LUK 9:28 parallels MAT 17:1, MRK 9:2

Jesus Christ is visibly transfigured

MAT 17:2 parallels MRK 9:2–9:3, LUK 9:29 The transfiguration was a glimpse of Jesus Christ's glory whereby his physical appearance was visibly changed.

The disciples are eye-witnesses of Jesus Christ's glory

MAT 17:6 See also MRK 9:9; LUK 9:32; JHN 1:14; 2PE 1:16

Moses and Elijah appear at the transfiguration

LUK 9:30–9:31 parallels MAT 17:3, MRK 9:4 “departure” refers to Jesus Christ's death.

God's voice is heard at the transfiguration

MAT 17:5 parallels MRK 9:7, LUK 9:35 God the Father's voice is heard authenticating Jesus Christ's ministry. See also MAT 3:17 parallels MRK 1:11, LUK 3:22; 2PE 1:17–1:18

Fear

LUK 9:36 parallels MAT 17:9–17:10, MRK 9:9–9:10 These verses reiterate that Jesus Christ's full glory was hidden from the world until his resurrection.

title: 2585: Jesus Christ, trial of
content:
Jesus Christ, falsely accused of blasphemy towards God and treason towards Rome, humbly endured his trial as a fulfilment of the purposes of God.

The preliminary hearing before Annas

See also JHN 18:12–18:14 Annas, though deposed from office by the Romans, was probably still regarded by many as the true high priest; JHN 18:19–18:23

The trial before Caiaphas and the Sanhedrin

Jesus Christ is sent to the ruling high priest

MAT 26:57 parallels MRK 14:53, JHN 18:24; ; LUK 22:54

False evidence is sought

MAT 26:59–26:60 parallel MRK 14:55–14:56 The Sanhedrin was the high court of the Jews and consisted of 71 chief priests, elders and teachers of the law, including the high priest who presided.

False accusations are made

See also MAT 26:60–26:61 parallel MRK 14:57–14:59 A person could only be convicted on the evidence of two or more witnesses.

Jesus Christ's trial centres upon his Messianic claims

MAT 26:65–26:66 parallel MRK 14:63–14:64; ; MAT 27:1 parallel MRK 15:1 The Sanhedrin decided that Jesus Christ deserved death but charged him with treason rather than blasphemy.

title: 2595: incarnation
content:
The assuming by God of human nature in the person of Jesus Christ. The incarnation is the fixed and permanent physical dwelling of God in his world, as opposed to the temporary manifestation of the divine presence and power in a theophany.

God the Son assumed human nature

JHN 1:14 See also JHN 1:9, JHN 8:56; 1TI 3:16; 1JN 1:1–1:2, 1JN 4:2; 2JN 1:7

Jesus Christ's incarnation involved a supernatural conception

LUK 1:35 See also ISA 9:6; MAT 1:18; JHN 14:9 The incarnation was such that the Son was the exact representation of the Father; ROM 1:4; COL 1:15, COL 1:19; HEB 1:2–1:3

Jesus Christ Chist's incarnation involved a virgin birth

MAT 1:22–1:23 See also ISA 7:14; LUK 1:34; ROM 1:3; GAL 4:4; PHP 2:8; HEB 2:14

The cost of the incarnation

PHP 2:6–2:7 See also 2CO 8:9; HEB 2:10 The incarnation involved Jesus Christ's suffering.

The necessity of the incarnation

ROM 8:3 See also ROM 5:17–5:19; 2CO 5:19; COL 1:22; 1TI 2:5 Only a person who is fully human and fully divine can be the effective mediator between God and humanity; HEB 2:17–2:18, HEB 4:15
